I have been divorced 3 times , which means I have had a lot of different attorneys represent me in trying to resolve some very difficult issues pertaining to divorce and children.  I must say that I was truly blessed to have found Salcido law firm and especially Jon Hogelin who took on my case as my attorney.
Prior to meeting with Jon, I felt my other  attorneys treated me like a number , and worst my cases as unimportant. I felt Jon treated me like family.  But most impressive was his ability to listen and remember what I was telling him and then  treat my case like it was for a family member or close friend. Jon reviewed and studied my case immediately in front of me and behind closed doors.  I know this to be true because he would tell me things( not discussed between us )that were very important to my case I had forgotten about.  Jon also would return my calls “quick” by calling or sending me a email or text which helped me with my anxieties of whether or not things were happening in a timely manner on my case , and also simple updates to my case.  He  even set a mediation up for me the day before a closing to a four year case he had been working on and still needed to after we left.   Don’t get me wrong Jon is a busy attorney,  but he  has the unique ability to somehow treat u as if you are his only client. In  the end he won my case for me  (from start to finish in less then 1 month) (prior attorneys were 8 months in and still no resolutions to my case or my anxieties ).   Jon Hogelin and Chris Salcido restored my faith in “SOME “ attorneys and lawyers out there truly trying.  Thanks Jon and Chris , I will forever be in your debt and always be a loyal customer!

He handled my cases with the utmost care and I felt confident throughout the entire process. His attention to detail and dedication to my cases was truly impressive. I would highly recommend him to anyone in need of legal assistance. He went above and beyond, and I couldn't be more satisfied with the outcome.

I was in an accident a little while ago. After the accident, I found Jacob Gunter, and he was an incredible advocate and support through the injuries and legal dealings. As someone who had never needed a lawyer before, I didn’t know what to do or how to advocate for myself. When I hired Jacob, I had no idea how much I would value his expertise and support during the process.

He went the extra mile multiple times to help me really understand the decisions I had to make and represented me very well.  He genuinely cares about his clients and works hard to get them justice.  If you want a lawyer who isn’t afraid to get you justice and help you feel comfortable throughout the process, Jacob is fabulous. If I ever need another lawyer (which I hope I don't), I would definitely go back to Jacob Gunter.
Jacob's expertise helped my family immensely during a highly stressful time; he's efficient, methodical, provides clear guidance, and delivers.

This is by far the worst attorney I have ever dealt with.  My daughter had an accident where she was rear ended by another driver.  The evidence in the case, if were presented correctly, easily proves that the driver that hit my daughter was absolutely operating her vehicle with negligence.  In a rear end accident, 99.9% of the time, you are operating with negligence if you rear end someone.  I've been in the insurance industry for 20 years and I have never seen a rear end accident where the driver was not found at fault by more that 80%.  This is one of the easiest cases for any attorney to win, and he failed.  He was ill-prepared for the case, so much so that in his opening arguments he didnt even have the address of the accident correct.  He failed to protect his client by making sure one of the most important witnesses, the responding officer to the accident, was available to take the stand.  He decided he was going to win the case with a few pictures of the damage blown up on canvas, and didnt need the officer or anyone else to prove negligence.

Furthermore, he told my daughter that if he didnt win the case, she wouldnt pay anything.  At the day of the trial, 2 years later, she finds out there is a counter claim if she loses and she would be responsible for it if he lost the case.  When telling us this, they said it would be a few hundred dollars at best, but they didnt believe it was going to be an issue. (I say they because he brought in another attorney at the last minute to help run the trial, and that guy wasnt worth much either)  It ended up being almost $1400 that she paid because he failed to do his job.  He refused to speak with her or myself after the trial.  He immediately dropped his name on the case.  When there were attempts to talk to him or his firm's partners, it was met with silence.  As soon as he lost the case, he ran to the shadows and disappeared.  He told us he would call and arrange a time to talk about the case and what happened, but he never did.

It has been over a year now, and with time to reflect on the case, because I wanted to make sure I was coming at this with the right frame of mind and emotion. I can say the Judge failed, he could have overturned what he knew to be an injustice.  The attorney representing the other driver, and her insurance Geico, lied and fabricated a story that was easily disproven with the facts of the case.  But at the end of the day, Jake didn't take the time or care to address any of this.  He refused to meet with my daughter and I during lunch breaks or recesses to strategize.  I have decided to write this review in hopes to keep others from making the same mistake.  My daughter still deals with back pains from the accident, and someday she will need a surgery to fuse the damaged discs in her back, and because of his failure to prepare and manage the case, she will be facing the expense of this on her own.

If I could I wouldnt even have given him one star, but they won't post this review unless I give at least one.  He might be good at managing cases out of court, but if he has to go to trial, it seems to me you would be better off with someone else for your representation.
reviews.ownerResponse: David, the jury just didn't believe your daughter or you.  The jury spoke.

reviews.ownerResponse:
It’s easy to post a review like this without understanding the full context on both sides. Because we are obligated to protect client confidentiality, we cannot discuss the details of our representation publicly.

However, we do need to address one important point: we will not tolerate mistreatment of our staff. During your two most recent calls, you were extremely rude, condescending, and abusive, to the point that you made members of our team cry. That conduct is unacceptable.

We are grateful to work with exceptional clients—including members of your immediate family—and we take pride in maintaining a respectful, professional environment for everyone. For that reason, we will not continue representing a client who shows such disrespect toward our staff.

We wish you the best of luck moving forward with your case.

reviews.text:
Last November, I found myself in a situation I never expected to face—being involved in a car accident. Initially, my plan was to handle everything through my insurance, believing that I could manage the situation on my own. However, after speaking with friends and coworkers who had endured similar experiences, I learned that seeking legal representation could make a significant difference. I hesitated at first, unsure if I truly needed an attorney, but I soon realized how crucial that decision would be.

In my search for an attorney, I came across We Win Injury Law (Troy, Jaren, Tyler and Ali). I am overwhelmingly grateful for that discovery. From the very first consultation, I was struck by their professionalism and deep understanding of the complexities surrounding injury claims. Their expertise took all the worry out of the claims process, allowing me to focus on what truly mattered: my healing and recovery.

One of the most remarkable aspects of working with We Win Injury Law was their extensive network of high-quality medical professionals. Thanks to their established relationships, I was able to access top-tier medical care with significantly reduced wait times. This not only expedited my treatment but also alleviated the stress that often accompanies such situations. I was able to concentrate on my recovery rather than stressing over appointments and long waitlists.

As it turned out, the individual who struck me was underinsured, leaving many of my costs uncovered initially. I thought I was fortunate to have purchased underinsured motorist coverage through my insurance company. However, I quickly learned that navigating the claims process could be arduous and frustrating. Thankfully, the attorneys at We Win Injury Law stepped in with tenacity and skill. They fought tirelessly on my behalf, ensuring that I received everything I was entitled to. Without their advocacy, I shudder to think of the struggles I would have faced on my own.

What truly set We Win Injury Law apart was their dedication to communication. They kept me informed every step of the way, breaking down the complexities of my case into manageable pieces. Their transparency and willingness to answer all my questions made an otherwise overwhelming process feel simple and structured. I never felt alone; I had a team of professionals who genuinely cared about my well-being and fought for my rights.

If you ever find yourself in a situation where you need legal assistance—whether due to a car accident or any other personal injury—I wholeheartedly recommend We Win Injury Law. They are more than just attorneys; they are advocates who empower you to reclaim your life after an unexpected setback. I can confidently say that I am in a much better place, both emotionally and physically, because of the choice I made to enlist their help.

BEWARE: Poor Communication, No Accountability, and Vague Billing

***l just got notified on Jan 30 2026 they posted an update to my review… 😂  but my review stands… terrible service…  look how long it took for them to respond to my review lol

BEWARE. I hired Sumsion Business Law hoping for professional legal help, but instead I experienced confusion, poor communication, and ultimately felt taken advantage of.

From the beginning, my case didn’t seem to be understood. The advice I received — including being told to ask the opposing party for a loan (!!) — made it clear they hadn’t grasped the facts. To put it mildly.

I decided to end the relationship and was told that any unused portion of my retainer would be returned after a final invoice.

But instead, I received a surprise bill for charges that supposedly exceeded my retainer — which I had been expecting a refund from.

When I asked for clarification, I was ignored. They claimed they had no meaningful contact with the other side — yet their advice to me was based on what that attorney supposedly said.

Since then, I’ve seen other recent reviews describing nearly identical experiences: poor communication, being ghosted after payment, and only getting responses once a public review was posted. That tells me this isn’t just bad luck — it looks like a pattern.

I'm disappointed in this service because I had high hopes when they first started helping me, only to see it spiral into miscommunication and now dishonesty in billing extremely excessive amounts.

I’m now pursuing formal complaints. I wouldn’t recommend this firm to anyone.
reviews.ownerResponse: Unfortunately, the firm’s candid assessment of unrealistic expectations may not always be appreciated. We strive to exceed REALISTIC expectations. If that is “poor accountability,”  we will own it

reviews.text:
My grandparents hired this attorney to help recover money from a former business partner who was embezzling funds and falsifying documentation. We paid this attorney expecting representation, advocacy, and help pursuing justice.

Instead, the entire experience amounted to two Zoom meetings. During those meetings, it felt like the attorney and the interpreter were more focused on helping the person who wronged my grandparents rather than representing our family’s interests. The interpreter was ineffective, communication was poor, and there was no meaningful progress or action taken on our case.

For the amount paid, the lack of effort, professionalism, and results was extremely disappointing. This was a stressful situation for my grandparents, and we trusted this attorney to help — that trust was not met.

I would not recommend this attorney based on our experience.

I went here looking for some advice for my company, and I got placed with Chase and Dallin the paralegal. They were both extremely helpful, and I truly believe they had my best interest at heart. They were able to help me solve some of my issues and help point me in the right direction the communication was prompt and they helped me without it costing me a fortune. I would recommend them to anybody.
Tyler Heap and Isaac Tanner were phenomenal. Both deserve a raise and a promotion.

Sumsion Business Law is truly incredible! Their team is sharp, experienced, and genuinely cares about their clients. Their attorneys are all professional, approachable, and extremely knowledgeable about business law. You will not regret it. They are the kind of firm that makes you feel taken care of from the very first conversation.

My wife and I own a small cleaning company in Northern Utah, which had been growing rapidly until a complicated legal matter devastated us a few months back. Thanks to Andrew Fellows and Sumsion Business Law we recently won a big victory in court that has ended our nightmare and saved our business. I also work as a legal assistant for a firm that takes family law and criminal law cases and I used to work for a company where I took calls for hundreds of different law firms across the US and Canada. On top of that, I am currently in law school myself. I know good legal work and professionalism when I see it. Andrew and his team were prompt and responsive. They were always conscious of our budget, which was admittedly very limited since we are such a small company. Even with that limitation however, Andrew was able to get us what I think was the best of all reasonable outcomes in court. I highly recommend working with this firm!

I had a very disappointing experience with attorney Aaron Wise during my case involving the Division of Child and Family Services (DCFS). After I requested to terminate his services, he continued to act as if I were still his client and sought authorization to file documents without my permission.

I have had the pleasure of working with Mr. Wiseman during two misdemeanor criminal defense cases while he was acting in the capacity of Public Defender for Springville Justice Court and I want to document the respect and gratitude I have for him. He has always been willing to proceed in accordance with my decisions, but only after making sure that he provided me with a clear understanding of the potential consequences of those decisions. In doing so, Mr. Wiseman has demonstrated his willingness and ability to act as a trial lawyer and even appellate lawyer when in the best interest of his client.

Whereas other public defenders often, in my experience, allow the sheer volume of their caseload to limit their strategy to negotiating plea agreements with prosecuting counsel, and advise clients against fighting charges, not because of the strength of the case against them, but because it is faster and easier to do so. Unfortunately, it is my opinion that the widespread prevalence of such practices has facilitated many local law enforcement officers' attitude of superiority toward the general public, and even toward the law they have undertaken an oath to enforce (...enforce, but apparently not abide by), routinely violating citizen's (defender's) Constitutional protections as matters of standard procedure because it has become apparent to them that their actions and testimony will be accepted prima facie (despite the fact that doing so places the accused of being guilty until proven innocent).

These attitudes and practices have a terrible effect on the general public's opinion of law enforcement, resulting in the utter polarization of law enforcement and minorities (in particular) all across the country. I have personally suffered the misfortune of having my Constitutional 5th Amendment Rights violated by law enforcement during an encounter in which I began as the victim of a violent crime and ended a suspect.

Sadly, when I sought to suppress the evidence obtained during the illegal search and seizure, the prosecuting attorney ensured me that if I stopped pursuing the suppression of the evidence, withdrew my claim of misconduct, and plead guilty to the charge, he would not seek any jail time at sentencing, instead recommending a combination of fines, treatment, and probation. However, he made it exceedingly clear that if I were to pursue the suppression of the evidence and thus went on record with my attorney interrogating the arresting officer about the "alleged" (and later accepted) violation of my rights, if I were found guilty, the prosecution would be asking the maximum sentence of six (6) months in jail be imposed. While I understand the nature and purpose of using plea bargains to save on legal costs, this felt more like the District Attorney attempting to protect the offending officer from having to admit any misconduct by threatening me with jail time if I pursued the matter.

As it happens, I believed that the circumstances of the case and relevant legal precedence were in our favor and so chose to pursue the appeal as a matter of principle. To show law enforcement that they cannot violate the laws they have sworn to protect just because they wear a badge, and to demonstrate that there are some individuals left who are willing to stand up against such abuse of power.

Luckily, after ensuring I was aware of the potential consequences of pressing the matter, Mr. Wise was happy to pursue the suppression of evidence based on violation of 5th Amendment Rights, and did an excellent job both preparing the requisite legal pleadings and interrogating the officer in such a manner as to support our Petition under oath. Long story short, the only evidence in the case was thrown out during appeal and the initial conviction was remanded. I couldn't be more pleased with Mr. Wise and am sure your experience would be similar.

reviews.text:
I highly recommend 100+ Stars. Kurt London, @ London Harker, went the extra 100 miles to make my life easier. I haven't been an easy client he had to put up with me for over 4 years of new Doctors, 2nd opinion Surgeons. Neck braces, back braces, Medication specialists, surgery after surgery and many more problems I've had to deal with, since my accident.
I know I'm not London Harkers only client but Kurt London, made me feel like I'm his ONE AND ONLY! And also made me feel like family. And in the end he won my case. Which made my life easier. Physically I will never be the same, but at least now I don't worry about money. If you ever need an attorney go to London Harker and have Kurt represent you.
Thank you
Shane
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you, Shane. It was a pleasure fighting for you on your case!

reviews.text: Scott and his team have been a blessing in my life.  I am so grateful that a good friend recommend him to help my husband and I in dealing with all the legal and medical aspects of a terrible car accident.  Scott was very caring, knowledgeable and with me throughout my healing and recovery stages.  Thank you so much for everything. Your the BEST!

Honest, knowledgeable and friendly people. Great service. My medical records were a mess after my car accident. They tried multiple times and ways to reduce the bills. They didn't quit with the first no. Very happy with the experience and results!

Scott did a great job with my car accident case! I was t-boned by another driver while making a right turn and I had a lot of medical bills. At first, the other insurance company tried to blame me for the accident, but Scott got involved and proved that the other driver was 100% responsible for my injuries. Scott and his team fought hard and got me a good settlement, and they even negotiated to get my medical balances reduced. I am very happy with the service I received from Scott. I would highly recommend hiring Scott.
Scott and his office is someone i have used more than once for my car accidents i recommend him for anyone who asks.  I wont go anywhere else for most things. he is awsome
Very knowledgeable and great service
Great atty
Great Lawyer. Very knowledgeable man, also very helpful as opposed to the attorney's that aren't really interested in giving you advise until you pay them lots of $$$.

Scott is an amazing attorney. He did a fantastic job fighting my cases and took care of everything. He made sure that I was recovering well and got the medical help that I needed.  He also would break things down for me and put it on my level so that I understood everything that was going on and wasn't left in the dark.
Very attentive and professional. I highly recommend him.
I wish I could post the video of the preliminary hearing, David you should be ashamed, it's in the hands of the bord of ethics now.
I recommend him, he is a good person, honest and helpful in everything.
Great first visit
Thank you David! I encourage anyone with legal needs should go this law firm. He put and end to my immigration saga!

Professional, caring, and responsible. He's always on top of the processes and keeps you updated on how they're going. He always responds to emails and calls when you have questions. A very professional lawyer who knows how to do his job well.
I have worked with Jeff and Steve for many years. They are honest and know thier stuff. They will really go to bat for you. I would trust them with anything.

The staff at Metler Law far exceeded my expectations. Several times throughout the process they called and texted to check my progress and see how I was feeling. They were very open and communicated clearly. I would definitely recommend them!

I’m a big fan of Brad and Kourtney. My family had been in a car accident and I didn’t know any of the legal lingo and what to expect and Kourtney was always keeping me updated on things and explained things I didn’t understand.  Brad did as well.  I felt like they were always looking out for my best interest which isn’t common now a days it feels like.  I recommend these guys big time!!

I spent over four years with Jeff handling my case and ended up with zero results. I eventually switched to another law firm, and they settled my case in just three months, for significantly more money than Jeff ever said was possible.

During those four years, I found myself doing most of the legwork, making calls, picking up and dropping off paperwork and got nothing to show for it except wasted time and lost money. I’m very disappointed that he still took payment from me and given the poor outcome. Be warned to expect long delays, minimal progress, and to do most of the work yourself.

I want to share my experience with anyone who is considering this firm. I was in and auto accident with my mother and children in the car. A friend of mine recommended Metler Law and I'm so happy that he did. They came to me for our first meeting and my case was assigned to Hope Johnson. She was awesome to work with! This firm is honest and upfront. Hope was responsive with communication and she worked her tail off to get me the best and most fair settlement possible. I sensed to dishonest from start to finish. I'd give them more than 5 stars if I could. If you're looking for a great injury settlement attorney I'd call them right now!

After an auto accident, I was told to hire a lawyer by 3 medical professionals.  After doing everything I have been told to do, Metlers office can only get me policy limits.  I would have gotten those any way without their fees.  In trying to understand why I cannot get ongoing medical paid or be reimbursed for lost wages, the attorney said he had another call and hung up on me.
They made everything so easy, and they were always very easy to get ahold of.

My husband was recently in an auto accident and I immediately knew I wanted to work with Metlers’ office. I previously worked at a chiropractic office that worked with auto related accidents all the time and we would see many patients with many different personal injury attorneys and by far metlers office was the nicest, genuine and most understanding office I’d seen in over 4 years working there. They were not only respectful (but persistent if they needed to be) to the offices they work with but genuine to the patients as well.
My husband had many questions throughout the process and Brad, Monica, and Courtney were always so understanding to speak with him.
We are grateful we got a larger settlement then we were expecting but also that we were able to close the case and Brad was able work with insurance quickly after my husbands treatment stopped in order to get our settlement back quickly because we had tapped into our savings to pay for medical bills.
All in all can’t recommend this office enough!

I was recently involved in a motorcycle accident, and upon my wife’s recommendation, we chose to work with MacArthur, Header & Melter. From the beginning, Monica, Brad, and Courtney handled our case with exceptional care and professionalism. They took the time to listen to our concerns and thoroughly answered all of our questions. Thanks to their hard work, all of our medical bills were taken care of, and we received a great settlement. It was clear throughout the process that they genuinely care about their clients and are dedicated to providing strong representation. My wife and I highly recommend their legal team.

Highly recommend. From the beginning they were very detail oriented and communicative. They set reasonable expectations throughout the whole process and were prompt to respond to any questions I had. They turned a very stressful situation into something manageable.

I wouldn’t recommend this lawyer. My case was dragged out unnecessarily, and I later found out I may have had a viable medical malpractice claim. But by the time someone else pointed it out, it was too close to the statute of limitations for anyone to take it. That should’ve been caught early on if someone had really been paying attention.I was also given medical advice, which no attorney should be doing—that’s not their job. They’re supposed to interpret medical records legally, not act like doctors. My parents also used him for a car accident case (against my advice); he didn’t fight for them at all, if my dad wasn’t so insistent they would’ve only had enough to pay the bills/fees. It really felt like our cases just weren’t worth his effort.

I have nothing but positive things to say. The entire experience was easy and they handled everything for me. They communicated along the way. Karla was wonderful to work with, she always kept in touch with me about what was going on and answered any questions that I have. They really helped me to make some good come out of this experience.

After my accident, I couldn't decide if it was right for me to hire an attorney. However, after a recommendation, I decided to hire Metler Law and it was the best decision I could have made. I never felt lost during the process because the Metler Law staff always kept my updated and was able to answer any and all questions I had. Thank you again to the team that handled my case!

The folks at Metler truly handled everything that needed to be done throughout the course of my case. They were very responsive, kind, and interested in me as a person. They always had time for me and my questions. Karla took me through all the paperwork and she gathered the details of the medical care I needed after my trip and fall accident. Brent took over when it came time to negotiate with the insurance company. His persistence and eye for details made it possible for me to get a very fair settlement.

Wonderful staff and excellent attorneys!! I can speak from experience that when you need someone you can absolutely count on to represent you in a positive and honorable way, with the greatest integrity, Jeff Metler and his awesome staff are the best! Thank you so very much for caring about me and my family.

I had the opportunity to work with Kourtney and Brad and they made me feel very involved and comfortable with the whole process. If I could give them a 100 star review I would! I will definitely be recommending Metler Law to anyone in need.

I would have been so lost without Brad and Courtney during the process of my case. Everyone at the office was wonderful to work with.
Brad handled my case so expertly and what felt like a minefield originally became very manageable for me. His experience as a talented  injury attorney was vital to my case. I feel so fortunate and was well taken care of.
Courtney always responded immediately to any question or request knowledgeably and professionally. She was so patient with me in my confusion in the early months after the accident. She is a genius paralegal in my opinion.
I would never want to go through something like this again, but if I did it would be with them.
My gratitude is deep and I won't ever forget the care I was given during a terrible time in my life.
Thank you for everything!

Absolute stellar work from Krissy who handled my case. I would recommend them to anyone and everyone they were always on top of things no matter what and everything was resolved quickly! They went above and beyond. Thank you again!!

Jeff and he’s team were awesome to work with. I worked with Jeff, Monica, then Brent. They worked diligently to get me my settlement to pay for my back surgery after my auto incident. Even with insurance being extremely difficult to work with, they were still able to get me the max settlement amount. I now have completed my surgery thanks to their efforts. I highly recommend!

The Jeff Metler team has been so easy to work with and helped make what was a stressful and painful situation a lot easier to deal with. I am so glad I reached out after my car accident to get their help with my claim. I have really appreciated their advocacy for me throughout the process.

Needing a lawyer is an emotional experience but Metler Law made it as positive of an experience as possible. They kept me posted of the process and progression. Thanks Metler Law for helping me through a difficult time and giving me the best outcome that could be had in my circumstances!
I had the pleasure of working on a case with Brent Johnson.  He is intelligent, responsive, and did a great job for his clients.  I'd recommend Brent and his firm to clients any time.
Great people to work with! Steve was very responsive and reassuring right off and Crissy did a great job keeping me informed and answering all my questions.
I would highly recommend Jeff Metler Law to any one. They walked us through the entire complicated process. The staff are all very friendly and knowledgeable.
Fantastic follow through! I recommend them to anyone! I received a great settlement and they went the extra mile!
Brent was very quick, efficient, and professional. Would definitely recommend them to anyone with a personal injury case.

I recommend the Metler Law group for any personal injury cases or anything else that they may be able to represent you in! The process for them to represent you is convenient and they stay in touch throughout the process. I always felt well informed of all the actions and given guidance on how to proceed. They got me the amount I needed to pay off all my expenses and some. I couldn’t be happier with my experience with this law firm and highly recommend them to anyone!!

The Metler group did an amazing job! They followed up constantly with updates but did everything on their end, no hassle on mine. I was referred to them by a friend and I would absolutely recommend their services. Thank you Jeff Metler Law!

I could not recommend Jeff Metler and staff more than I am about to right now. Easiest process, they drove to meet me for convenience, kept in contact with me throughout the process and most importantly- minimal effort from us. They got us our maximum settlement for our car accident that happened last year and really represented us and our needs in the most professional manner. Thank you Brent and Karla for everything you have done for us. 11 out of 10, 6 stars- thank you!!!

This lawyer is a negotiator, not a lawyer.  Lawyer fight cases melter just asks the insurance to pay if they don’t metler will say it’s a conflict of interest and wastes your time.  They are fake and need to learn better law.  I have a case where the police report says it the other guys fault and metler couldn’t completely the case.  Now I gotta pay for everything out of pocket and get a better lawyer and sue the other guy.  DONT EVER USE THIS MAN.  HES JUST LOOKS LIKE A LAWYER BUT DOEANT ACT LIKE ONE.

It was an absolute pleasure working with Metler Law. They were very informative of the process, answered all of my questions and kept in constant communication every step of the way. Their involvement allowed me to stay focused on getting the care I needed to deal with my injury and not worry about financial matters related to accident. Metler Law was successful in obtaining a fair settlement and went above & beyond to negotiate with the providers to maximize the most possible compensation back to me.  I would highly recommend their professional services to anyone looking for a great firm to represent their interests in an auto accident.
Professional. They kept in touch with me every month to give me updates. They did a great job.

Metler law specifically April and Brent were so easy to work with. They did a  AMAZING job! This was my first time needing an attorney for anything and it went better than I could have hoped for! They were professional and explained everything to me and I greatly appreciated their help and time. Their only concern was me and my injuries and made sure I was getting better. Hands down best experience I could have ever hoped for. I wouldn’t hesitate one second to call them again if I ever needed anything. I can’t say thank you enough!!!!

Jeff Metler and his team have gone above and beyond for me. I experienced a car accident while DoorDashing June of 2021. The whole process I was kept in touch and in the loop, which I greatly appreciate. I have to mention Brett because he was a huge help.
I can't remember his name, (please correct me if I'm remembering wrong) but I want to say Steve drove out to me two times to help me with paperwork. He's afraid of dogs...I work with dogs, so I greatly appreciate his dedication to his job and his amazing customer service!
Very professional and quick to respond whenever I had questions. 10/10 would recommend using them.
They are the best team and fought for me to get me what I deserve Jeff,April,Brent thank you all again for helping me with my case I will definitely recommend you  😉👍

absolutely amazing I felt 100% heard and I didn’t have to lift a finger. Monica (case manager) worked endlessly on my case and called me with any updates she had to put my mind at ease 100% recommend.
These guys are amazing, they really took the time needed to make sure my case was settled fairly, and even when I got impatient, they were always very patient with me.
Hope was amazing to work with, and helped me close down a case and communicated with me greatly and honestly throughout the process.

Metler Law was so extremely easy to work with! I am shocked at how much money they were able to get us for our auto accident. They were very friendly and honest during every step of the process. They made us feel important. I would highly recommend sitting down with them and consulting on your issue. They are so good at what they do and truly care about their clients. We are so appreciative!

Outstanding service. Made my suit so much easier and my life so much easier. They actually cared about me as a person and wanted to make sure I was ok first.

They also got me way more then I thought I would!!!!!
I work with this office closely on a daily basis, so when my husband was in an accident on his way to work in May…I immediately called them to get him signed up
Very professional and understanding. Helped us through the process and didn't mind answering so many questions. Fast settlement. Already recommended our friends. Pleased with the outcome.

They were genuinely real, and honest at all times. Without a doubt I will come to them again if there is any need. (2017)

Ironically enough, my wife and I were in a car accident about a year and a half ago after settling with my motorcycle accident. AGAIN Jeff took care of us and ensured that we were taken care of. Fingers crossed UTAH Driver’s improves and there’s not another review update. (2019)

reviews.text:
We needed a construction attorney for a Utah real estate project. Attorney Jeremy Eveland played a pivotal role in resolving a complex construction lien issue for our general contractor project in Holladay. His deep understanding of construction law and his proactive approach were instrumental in navigating through the challenges we faced. Mr. Eveland’s expertise was apparent as he guided us through the intricacies of lien law, ensuring compliance and protecting our interests.

He was interested in finding practical solutions for me. He helped in negotiations in resolving the problem quickly. Throughout the process, he responded timely to emails, keeping us informed of developments and providing clear, actionable steps. We also talked on the phone nearly every week for updates. His professionalism and dedication are exceptional, and his efforts resulted in a successful resolution for us. Working with Jeremy for us was a great experience.

Jeremy Eveland helped with our estate plan in Provo. From the initial consultation to the final signing, Jeremy demonstrated knowledge and a genuine commitment to understanding our family’s unique situation. He patiently walked us through the estate law, ensuring we covered the necessary documents and processes for our estate plan. He anticipated potential issues and provided clear solutions and explanations when I didn't understand the what or the why of it. I also liked his personalized approach. He took the time to listen to our concerns and goals, making the legal documents for our specific wishes. I feel like Jeremy's not just my lawyer, but my friend.
Jeremy helped me with an estate plan. He did a will, trust, power of attorney and health care directive. Because of him, I feel like my estate is secure.
I needed help from a probate lawyer. Jeremy not only knew what steps needed to happen, but he carefully walked me through each one. So grateful for his help.
Jeremy took the time to talk with me and gave me a little more peace of mind. Great guy, empathetic and would hire him in a heartbeat.

reviews.text:
Do NOT use this firm for your bankruptcy. Derek and his team have horrible communication skills. They make careless mistakes and when it’s brought to their attention, they either don’t respond at all or brush you off and still don’t make a correction. I signed a reaffirmation agreement for my auto loan, it was rejected by the courts because Derek didn’t sign the paperwork (careless mistake 1). When I brought the rejection letter to their attention they ignored me for weeks. When they finally responded I was told to ignore it and that it was handled. 2 months later I follow up on it because it’s still be rejected by the court. Derek asks me why it was rejected?? As my lawyer, he should have known this, but also I sent them the rejection later 2 months earlier and was assured this was handled.
Now I’m waiting on my report of no distribution from the trustee. The trustee requested documents back in March of 2025. I sent the documents I had in and informed them I could not access the other documents. They said I might have to sign an affidavit but that it’s fine. We are now in September and the trustee is still requesting the same documents because Derek’s firm had not communicated what I said back in March. I am now on week 3 trying to get a follow up and have gotten zero responses. Save yourself the extra stress in an already stressful situation and find a different lawyer.

Ron has been my attorney for a little over two years as I navigated a highly contentious divorce. I’ve also worked with his associate, Jennifer closely and often. They have been an absolute dream team. They have always looked out for the best interest of my kids and I and did everything in their power to get us through this process and into a better place so we can heal and move forward. They were always prepared, listened to my experiences and concerns, explained things well, and were often available quickly when situations would arise unexpectedly but needed attention.  Ron is wonderful at balancing the line of keeping peace and avoiding conflict when possible, but being aggressive and an advocate when he needs to be. Every time we were in court he was prepared, quick with information and answers, and aware of even the smallest details of my case. I had complete confidence in him in the courtroom, which brought me some peace in very stressful situations. Jennifer is adept in the legal world and has always been able to answer my questions and help me understand the process. But the most beautiful thing about her is she can do that while still holding space for compassion and empathy for the ways this experience affects a person emotionally. That’s not an easy combination to find in an attorney. Ron and Jennifer saw me as a person, not just as another one of their many clients. They trusted me as much as I trusted them and believed in me during the times it was hard for me to believe in myself. They guided me with great legal advice while still allowing me to trust my gut, they always had my back. As things are finally coming to a close, I am infinitely grateful that I had them in my corner during the hardest part of my life. Heritage Law lived up to its name, because the ways they helped me and my kids will undoubtedly have a positive ripple effect in our family for many generations to come.
reviews.ownerResponse: It is an incredible honor to represent such a capable and compassionate woman who was always prepared to consider options and put her kids first. Thank you so much for your kind and thoughtful words. They mean the world!

reviews.text:
Top Dollar for minimal effort.
Ron was often in court and unavailable unless a call was scheduled a week in advance. When I approached him about his unavailability, he replied telling me was getting daily correspondence from his legal aid. I asked numerous questions that were left unanswered. The lack of legal guidance drew out my divorce and left me alone to make decisions I wasn't comfortable making.
If you are looking for legal advice, Ron isn't your guy.
If you are looking for someone to push paper work you can find a Lawyer at a lower cost.

**Update on my review.
I spoke with Ron and we went over some specifics from my case. It was unfair of me to neglect to mention the good parts of my experience with Heritage Law. My paralegal was kind and really showed up in her effort to help me. And when things got rough we were able to make it to a safe place.
I switched Lawyers after I asked several questions that remained unanswered and I didn’t get the Response that I felt I needed to make educated decisions.
When I switched Lawyers I did find one who was much more available and would jump on calls with me and I felt like he really put effort in knowing my case.
Both wanted to push for mediation which is very expensive and where most cases end. You will have to pay for your lawyer fees and the mediator and it will likely take minimum half a day.
In my case I needed advice and direction, and Ron didn’t fill that need.
However, I did sit in court and I saw him in action, from what I could tell he is very competent  and well versed.
If you know your case is going to be a big deal and you need really good representation Ron will probably do great. If you need advice and someone to walk you through the process, I would look up Brown Family Law, they helped me find my other lawyer and the emails they sent me were the most helpful of all.
Also, if you are required to take classes, take them early, those were super helpful as well. I wish I would have taken them earlier.
reviews.ownerResponse: Most experienced, respected lawyers are not available to answer questions nearly every day.  I am glad you found an attorney who was better fit for your needs.

reviews.text: They seemed very nice and concerned for their clients.

reviews.text: I waited to do this review until our case wrapped up so I could give it the energy it deserves.  Ron and Nate are the best attorneys I have ever seen.   My husband and I have worked with several attorneys in the past for various reasons and we were so extremely impressed with them both.  Nate is a wonderful attorney.  He is extremely intelligent in writing and drafting documents.  He is very knowledgeable and extremely helpful.  He is always so willing to take a call and return our calls and was a great support.  Ron is an incredible person and super star attorney.  He has such a fun, happy demeanor that even when you are going through the hardest time, he had a smile on his face which somehow eases the stress of court.  He is positive and also realistic. He is strong when he needs to be and soft when he needs to be, and he will fight for you.  There were times he dropped everything to help us.  He stayed after hours.  He changed appointments.  I know he is very busy, but we felt like he truly cared about us, and we felt so lucky to have found him.  He is extremely savvy in the courtroom.  He reads the client, the defendant, and the judge as well as opposing counsel and will actually switch what he needs to so he can adjust to what is going on.  He is so sharp and would know what quick remark to make to a comment from opposing team to help our case. Ron knows the law better than anyone!  He is amazing at what he does, and he and Nate are the best attorney team I have ever witnessed. The staff are amazing as well.  Sara was so helpful when we needed things done quickly.  She was quick to respond and very helpful.  The front desk staff is very kind and good at what they do!  After working with this team for several months I can assure you that you are in the best possible hands.   Ron and Nate, we can’t thank you enough for fighting for us.  Thank you for taking this long 4-year journey we had and helping us get these issues solved so quickly and efficiently after so many years!
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for your kind words.  It is an honor to know you and work with your wonderful family.

She wasted her time, her gas and paid $150 for a consultation and they KNEW they wouldn't take her case. She is a newly single mom, fighting her ex who won't pay a dime of child support and they stole that money from her. Because attorneys need that money more than she does? My blood boils when I think of how they have taken advantage of her. They also sent her an email saying they were too full to take on her case. Again. WHY SCHEDULE THE INITIAL CONSULTATION??? She had 30 days to respond to being served. She took a little time to find someone she THOUGHT would be good and they scheduled her 2 weeks out, telling her there would be plenty of time to respond. By the time they got back to her, she had a few days left to pick a new attorney and everyone else was booked out several weeks. Stay away from these crooks. She emailed them to ask why they would put her in this situation and they have ghosted her!
reviews.ownerResponse: We regret to hear that your sister did not have a positive experience with our firm. Your expressed concern is appreciated and we will do better in the future to make the terms of a consultation clear. The purpose of a consultation is to determine if we can take a case on, which includes cases outside of Utah County, and if it would be a right fit for both the attorney and the client. Our caseload fluctuates and in order to meet the needs of the case, it was determined that a different firm would be a better fit. As stated in our confirmation email and consultation fee agreement, consultations do not guarantee representation. We hope the referrals we provided to your sister were useful and we wish her luck.

reviews.text: Myself, husband and our 5 kids were in an accident back in June 2025. A family member recommended Parker & McConkie to help us deal with all the matters of our accident. Our lawyer Brooklyne was amazing and got us setup with all the proper Dr’s needed for our recovery immediately. We got in to see a Dr very fast and began all the steps needed such as a pain pro specialist, chiropractor and physical therapy. 2-3 months later we finished all our treatments and are feeling much better than we felt before the accident. We were in a rental vehicle at the time of the accident. Brooklyne worked tirelessly and was able to get the party at fault to cover all the damages. The vehicle as well as our medical bills. We also got a settlement. I honestly don’t know how we would have been able to do any of that without Brooklyne. My family couldn’t have asked for a better lawyer to assist us. If you or anyone you know has been in an accident I highly recommend to have Parker & McConkie represent you. Absolutely thankful to have had them represent our family.
reviews.ownerResponse:

reviews.text: I highly recommend using Mckell Thompson and Hunter for your attorneys. I never thought I would ever need to use an attorney before but then I was in a car accident on my way to college. To make matters worse I was using a ride share to save money on gas and was not the driver. The drivers insurance told me they would not pay the full balance on my Medical bills that happen due to the car accident and was left with the bills. Eventually they got sent to collections because I was a poor college kid who could not afford both tuition and a big medical bill. Then I got Mike Mckell and his team involved, they were able to make the collection calls stop while they worked on settling my case. They were able to help me find a chiropractor who would agree to wait to get paid until after my case was resolved. Then along the way of resolving my case they kept me informed with any updates they hand and also willing to answer any questions. They made me feel important to them and treated me like family.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for your kind review. We enjoyed working on your case!

So I called this office and explained my situation, they answered all my questions, gave me peace of mind and assured that I would be helped if needed. I cannot express my appreciation and sense of ease after talking to the attorneys here. If I ever need a attorney I will call them and strongly urge you to do the same.

During my medical “work up” the doctor found a brain tumor (meningioma), the medical team (Dr Colin McDonald at Granger Medical), I was seen at Summit Brain and Spine the next morning. Things have been really hard since my car accident and Good Guys aren’t just my legal team, they are friends. The even helped me find legal advice when my tenant set our house on fire. I can’t express how much I value the legal team at Good Guys!! Amber is amazing at what she does!!! My recovery has been exhausting, I cannot imagine how much harder it would be if I didn’t have Amber and David! They will forever make our “Christmas card list”. I wouldn’t trust another law firm!

reviews.text: Excellent service!!
My experience has been nothing but amazing. The response is quick, and communication with Chris and his team is straight forward and helpful.
Brixton was an amazing attorney and I highly recommend him.

Brixton Hakes is excellent, highly recommend. He is  friendly, understanding, patient with a fun personality..
HIGHLY RECOMMEND !!

Only reason there is a one star rating is because that is the lowest rating.
These guys made us pay $3000 after being told the cost was $2800. The price went up after we had made the agreement for services.
We had no meetings, nor spoke with the attorney assigned to us, "Brixton". I emailed our situation and was told it was recieved.
But we had no contact with this guy until 15 minutes before court. He charged in with his tight, shiny, fancy suit, slicked back hair, and huge countenance of arrogance. Then did a bunch of fancy talking, completely disregarded what we wanted to get out of going to court. Didn't stand up for us AT ALL. Automatically took the deal from the opposing attorney, rushing us to quickly decide and not have any time to ask questions or talk options over with him.
Walked into court with us. Quickly did the court procedure and walked out of the court room and rushed off.
He had absolutely no regard as to our feelings about the "decision". He just wanted the money in exchange for doing the least amount of work possible.
$3000 for an attorney who we had never spoken to, didn't listen to our needs/wishes, was in our presence less that 30 minutes.
What a waste of money. Money that we had to scrape together because we didn't HAVE $3000 to spare.
In hind sight, Don't hire these guys, they will sweet talk you all day long. They make money off of a "well known name". They may have been a great place to hire years ago. But they don't care about WHO they are working for. They only care about the MONEY they get .
My advice. Find a small firm with someone humble enough to listen to you and talk to you about your options, at least 24 hours or more before rushing into court. And someone who is willing to actually EARN the money you pay them to do what you NEED done.
Side Note: A month ago I was getting on the freeway in Utah county. This cyber truck with a trendy "Dexter and Dexter" wrap ALL over it went weaving way too fast in and out of everyone on the onramp and did the same thing on the freeway. Going very very fast. But as the cyber truck passed by me, I noticed the license plate. BRIXTON. Figures.
Don't be fooled by the fancy twist of the facts. Crooks are always good at doing that.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Tammy, we are sorry to hear you were not satisfied with the complete Dismissal of the Protective Order that Chantel Hopkins filed against you over a year ago. Such a Dismissal is the best possible outcome possible. We would, however, like to address the issues you have raised. When we first spoke on September 1, 2023, we quoted you a fee of $3,480 and discounted the fee to $2,980 which you paid. There was never any deviation or increase from the amount we quoted and the amount you paid. Furthermore, we never asked for more money, and you never paid any more money.

We requested that you send us an email detailing your side of the story. Not only did you do that, but you sent numerous character reference letters as well. We thoroughly reviewed all of the information you provided. We then made it a point to discuss the matter with you the day before your hearing (Brixton included). When Brixton appeared prepared to argue your hearing, he was able to convince the opposing side to agree to a Dismissal of the case in its entirety. You were presented with this option and chose to accept that outcome. It was not Mr. Hakes who accepted the offer to dismiss the case, but rather it was you. In open court, you stated on the record that you heard, understood, and agreed with the terms of the Dismissal. At no point in time did you ever state that you wanted to argue the case on the merits instead of accepting the Dismissal. Again, we are sorry you were not satisfied with our efforts to successfully a dismiss the Protective Order brought against you.

We apologize for the driving discourtesy. There is no excuse for that and we commit to do better. In any event, we wish you the best going forward.

reviews.text:
Why drive a Cyber Truck? It might not be the best idea to have your company name plastered on the side of the most controversial car ever made. This lawyer seems completely out of touch with reality and public perception. If he is this disconnected from reality, is he more focused on your case, or his own delusions of reality?

The alternative is that this is 5D chess by an expert lawyer. That he knows the cyber truck is the most dangerous car with the most recalls in the world, made by the richest man in the world. As a personal injury lawyer he would know how to juice the crap out of a settlement WHEN, not IF, it fails.

Split in the middle by two equally absurd propositions. It’s up to you all to decide.

reviews.ownerResponse: We understand that family court matters are deeply emotional, especially when children are involved. Sandra’s role, as a Special Master and mediator, is governed by court orders and the law, and she does not have authority to make or override custody decisions. In mediation, the parties themselves come to decisions, which are respected. We recognize how difficult these situations can be for families and wish you the best.

reviews.ownerResponse:
Corinn, we are saddened to hear that this was your experience. We do not recognize your name and are not aware of the specific matter you are referencing, however, we understand that family law matters are deeply emotional, and it is not uncommon for parties to feel frustrated or hurt by outcomes they disagree with.

As a Special Master and mediator, Sandra’s role is not to favor one party over another, but to apply the law, court orders, and available information as impartially as possible, always with the best interests of the children in mind.

Sandra remains committed to conducting her work professionally, ethically, and in accordance with the law. We wish your family the best moving forward.

reviews.text: Sandra is an excellent mediator and extremely professional.
She wasn't honest with me and sent me information about another case. I would highly recommend you find someone else to help you with your legal issues.
reviews.ownerResponse: I apologize, but we do not recognize your name and have no record of any situation in which information may have been inadvertently shared. We take confidentiality very seriously and are committed to maintaining the highest level of professionalism in all matters. If you would like to contact our office directly, we would be happy to answer any further questions you may have.

reviews.text: Terrible ! completely bias to one party berated into submission to the other parties wishes took 11 hours to accomplish anything and even then it's like she was completely oblivious to the circumstances surrounding the case do your self a favor use someone that has the ability and competence to educate them selves on the case their working utterly ridiculous
reviews.ownerResponse: We understand family law mediations can be emotional and challenging. Longer sessions often reflect Sandra’s commitment to thoroughly addressing all matters and ensuring both parties are heard. We wish you the best.

reviews.text: Sandra is one of the most professional and hard-working lawyers I have had the privilege of knowing.
Sandra is an excellent mediator and Special Master. I would recommend her to anyone.

It would have been nice before my daughter’s first session to have been told what financial documents to have available so that precious time and money could have been saved. Because we didn’t have everything, we had to have a second session, in which she went over and over everything that we had resolved in the first  session.  We then ended up needing a third session, which she ghosted us. Several emails and no response. We went with a different mediator with greater and more quick success.
reviews.ownerResponse: I apologize you had a negative experience. We work hard to make sure our clients have the information they need. It seems like there was a disconnect as we reached out several times to resolve the situation.

reviews.text: Sandra Dredge is a very poor mediator. I would not recommend for mediating high stress divorce cases. Our second mediation took over 11 hours and resulted in Sandra adding more fluff into our original decree to serve as clarification to our parenting plan. This clarification fluff that was added has not served me as a parent and I expect it will actually lead to more disagreements from the opposing party in the future. Sandra came across very demeaning to woman and several times referred to our child as the opposing parties child. She did not fulfill her role as a mediator well and spent most of her time more as a special masters influence. She was overpayed for what was actually accomplished throughout the 11 hours we attended mediation and I still do not have an understanding of why we actually needed to attend mediation for a second time to begin with.
Sandra dredge was a poor excuse for a mediator. She let me be cornered by the opposing team and allowed injustice before her very eyes, would not recommend her services to anyone.
What a disgusting experience.  Possible to give negative stars?  Does law rule or wannabe "masters"?
reviews.ownerResponse:
Julie, I am sorry you feel that way. We are still on your case and have been for many years. If you had negative feelings, I wish you would have reached out to me or my staff personally. Please know I do my best to support all Special Master clients for the entirety I am on their case.
Regards,
Sandra.

reviews.text: I was very pleased with Sandra Dredge, she does a fabulous job. She is professional, empathetic, and straight forward. Her mediation expertise is invaluable.

Sandra is easily the best lawyer I've ever worked with over the years. She went above and beyond to help me fight an extremely crooked Worker's Compensation insurance company. I was constantly being given the short end of the stick until Sandra got involved. She has that southern "don't mess with me" attitude and that's exactly what I needed. I personally question the reviews less than 5 stars here because my experience was nothing but professional and exceptional. Everything turned out best case scenario for me despite rotten insurance company politics. I'd highly recommend anyone to Sandra without hesitation. I most certainly wouldn't be where I am today without her knowledge and assistance.

Sandra has been nothing but patient, kind, and honest  while working with me through my case. She has taken the time to review records, listen to my concerns, offer advice, and spend countless hours representing and fighting for me and what is best for my situation. I appreciate the work she has done and continues to do. I would highly recommend Sandra to anybody who needs help navigating a long and difficult case.

reviews.text: I’ve used Sandra on several mediations and have had success in every one of them. Sandra is a hands on mediator and can see the issues clearly. She is truly neutral in her approach. Having a scribe write everything down is a plus. Both parties leave with a hard copy of a stipulation. A digital copy is sent to the attorney who is going to prep the final docs.

ZERO STARS!!!

Where to begin?  Lazy, arrogant, careless.... Order of priorities seemed to be:
1. Making his day as easy as possible.
2. Extracting money from clients
3. Fostering relationships with opposing counsel, mediators, and etc.
4. Possibly helping get an acceptable outcome for clients

A couple examples of my experience:

I got destroyed at my Temporary Orders Hearing because Jason's office submitted my Financial Statement to the court without my signature.

Jason pressured and convinced me to sign a document neither he or I had read. I tore it up at the recommendation of his associate.

In summary, Jason was by far the worst "professional" I have ever had the displeasure of doing business with. Another attorney recommended that I consider suing for malpractice.

Be careful!!!

This law office takes care of business. They handle your case with professionalism and gusto. They are well worth their weight in gold and fight for their clients. You always can get a hold of somebody and they go above and beyond.

I had no experience with lawyers until my divorce so I didn’t know what good or bad looked like until late in my divorce process. I am now living year in and year out with the consequences of the very poor representation I received from Jason. He was consistently late for my hearings, didn’t seem to know my case, often seemed intimidated by opposing counsel, was disorganized, gave no or poor advice during the process, and did not communicate with me unless I initiated the contact. Even though I paid Jason over 20k, I got stuck with very high alimony and no contact with my children. Considering Jason, you should keep looking, you can do much better.

reviews.text:
I am extremely grateful for Chris Keen and his immigration team. My case was not simple — I had a wrongful appropriation charge and a criminal mischief case in my record, and I was very worried about how that would affect my U.S. citizenship process.

Chris fought for me every step of the way. He took the time to understand my situation, explained everything clearly, and built a strong strategy to address my past charges. Thanks to his knowledge, dedication, and experience, those issues were properly handled and I was able to move forward successfully with my citizenship.

If you have a complicated case and need an attorney who truly cares and knows immigration law inside and out, I highly recommend Chris Keen. He made a stressful situation manageable and helped me achieve one of the most important goals of my life — becoming a U.S. citizen.

reviews.text: Words cannot express my gratitude for finding you. We had spoken with two other lawyers, and they were completely useless. From the very first moment, we were treated with respect and consideration. You were the oasis we needed. It was so incredible that you delivered the package on December 23, 2025, and on January 5, 2026, we received approval, and my green card is already on its way! We got the answer in just a few days! From the bottom of my heart, I want to thank you for everything you did for us in record time. As soon as I receive my green card, I will upload photos. A special thank you to Zane Weeks, Bryson Taylor, Isaac Watts, and the rest of the team who supported us... You are the best!

reviews.text:
After a disappointing experience with a previous law firm that left us feeling lost and hopeless, working with Keen Law was a complete turning point. Our immigration case was fairly complex, and we were starting to lose hope until we found Keen Law.

From day one, their team showed a level of professionalism, dedication, and expertise that immediately put us at ease. They took the time to understand the full history of our case, corrected past mistakes, and developed a clear, strategic plan to move forward. Their communication was excellent, and they were always available to answer questions and offer reassurance throughout the process.

Thanks to their hard work our case was approved. Something I started to fear wasn't possible after our previous experience. We are forever grateful to Keen Law for not only getting us through this but for doing so with compassion and integrity.

If you’re dealing with a difficult immigration case, I wholeheartedly recommend Keen Law. They made all the difference in our lives.

reviews.text:
Dear The Good Guys Injury Law Firm,

I am writing to express my sincere gratitude for your exceptional service. I cannot say enough good things about your firm.

I was not expecting to pursue a claim against my own auto insurance, and navigating insurance policies proved to be painstakingly difficult, especially when I didn't fully understand my own coverage. I was repeatedly misinformed by multiple agents from my insurance company, who claimed I only had the state minimum medical coverage. Knowing something was amiss, I contacted several injury law firms, but most simply advised me to continue communicating with my insurance provider.

Ken was the only one who went above and beyond by asking me to send him my auto policy to verify my coverage. It turned out I was indeed covered for more than the $3,000 state-mandated minimum. Ken immediately assured me that he could take on my insurance company to ensure all my medical costs were covered and that I would be compensated for pain and suffering.

Ken and his team promptly began working on my case. While the chiropractic care provider you initially referred me to was excellent, I felt it wasn't the right fit for me, and your team graciously worked with me to choose my own physical therapy provider. Amber was meticulous with every detail and truly made me feel comforted and valued with every phone call and text, ensuring my recovery was progressing well and that I was being taken care of.

I also greatly appreciated the unexpected treats, the complimentary movie night, and the jump starter kit for my car as a goodbye gift. These thoughtful gestures truly set your firm apart from every other injury law firm I've encountered. You genuinely go all out for your clients and make frustrating situations less stressful.

Additionally, Amber referred me to Matt Cernyar at Goosehead Insurance, who was invaluable in helping me navigate the stressful process of purchasing new auto insurance, as I will be discontinuing my current policy due to the misinformation I received. Matt was also amazing, and I cannot recommend him enough.

The final settlement was more than I had anticipated and was more than fair.

Thank you again for everything.

Sincerely,
Jackie O.
reviews.ownerResponse: Jackie -- we're humbled by and appreciative of the time and effort you put into your review of our firm! Ken, Amber and the rest of the team do a great job at going the "extra mile" for our clients, and we're so pleased those efforts were noticed and appreciated by you! It was an honor to navigate through this process with you, and we are thankful for your trust and confidence, and how communicative you were with us throughout!

As soon as I got out of the hospital I contacted Good Guys Injury Law. They were with me through the entire process of doctor follow-ups and physical therapy for 5 months. They completely took care of dealing with doctor and hospital bills and dealing with the insurance companies. All I had to do was send them the bills as they came in. I was able to focus on recovery and not be stressed in the least with bills and insurance adjusters.

Both Mindi and Ken were reassuring and made me feel very at ease. They gave me regular updates on the claim process and they worked diligently toward my best interest.

Hopefully no more accidents but if there are, I will definitely call Good Guys Injury Law. I highly recommend their services to anyone injured in an accident.

reviews.text:
I had the privilege of getting helped by Rob at PacLegal a couple years ago.

There was an incident where I was being accused by the Pleasant Grove police of something I didn't do. The officer in charge apologized to me "off the record" indicating that he was just having a bad day and that he didn't intend for me to get arrested or charged. He suggested that if I came in the following day he could get something worked out.

Unfortunately, after my experience that day I didn't trust how they would behave and didn't feel comfortable being alone with them again. So I decided to call Pac Legal.

They immediately went to work gathering info on what happened. They requested footage from the vehicles and body cams and we were told that the footage had mysteriously vanished. This left us with a situation of 1 guy's word (i.e. mine) against 4 police officers who's reports all echoed the same misrepresentation the police officer apologized for "off record".

Sadly attempting to get the footage, instead of trusting that the officer would clear me, made them double down on charges. I was facing some serious trouble and Pac Legal was able to get the city and court to agree to a plea in abeyance and a small fine.

While I'd have hoped for a verbal apology on channel 4 news from each of the officers, I knew that wasn't possible. So I was grateful to PacLegal for helping to put the right pressure on the city to prevent the jail time and much larger fines I was facing...which would have included the long term problems that come with a criminal record.

Instead, I was able to get back to running my business. PacLegal even reached out when the time had passed to let me know that my record was now clear again.

They really do take a personal but professional approach to mitigate an otherwise impossible situation.
reviews.ownerResponse: Glad to help. Your case was extremely unfair and the police completely overreacted. I'm glad we could get it resolved appropriately.

reviews.text:
Hindsight is 20/20 but my lawyer at the time did not really seem to do much. When a protective order was brought up against me, he didn't even ask me for my side of the story, so I ended up pleading no contest on a protective order that I now feel was very easy to disprove. Keep in mind I was extremely distraught at the time.

The day the mediation was scheduled we went back and forth with her lawyer multiple times so the decree would be signed before meditation. They were obviously doing this because her lawyer knew that I was being taken advantage of, but my lawyer didn't even really fight for anything on my side.

Also, my last payment to him was through venmo which may be nothing but also seems a bit weird.

Long story short: I feel I was taken advantage much more than I should have been due to negligence of my lawyer.

We have been involved in some complicated court matters and Chris has always been patient with my questions, frustrations and lack of legal knowledge. We are very grateful some of our family members are now safe. Helping me see the ins and outs of the legal landscape is very helpful!
Mr. Greenwood treated me more than just another case. I am very grateful for all that he has done for my daughter and I.
Very good attorney.. I have worked with Chris as opposing counsel and I have associated with Chris as co-counsel and I would highly recommend Chris Greenwood with any legal matter.
A really rude entitled person

Go with somebody else, choose an ethical attorney!

Very friendly, knowledgable, and helpful.

reviews.text: If I could give them 100 stars I would. We were hit by a driver under the influence and 4 out of 5 of us ended up being taken by ambulance to the hospital. Initially we weren’t planning on getting a lawyer, but a friend recommended Moxie Law, and can I say it was the best decision we made. There were so many medical bills, doctors visits and car insurance claims that needed to be dealt with as a result of the accident, not to mention a legal case with the other driver. There is a zero percent chance I would have been able to navigate it on my own. They took a stressful and crappy situation and made it so smooth. They also went to bat for us with the insurance companies and walked us through the court case. They took a really tough situation and made it bearable. reviews.text:
Update to original review below dashed line: You're welcome for the review, but thanking me for my feedback means little unless it's followed by meaningful action. If you truly take these comments as seriously as you claim, then I encourage you to follow through on what you said you would do and deliver the results you were so confident about. If that happens, I’ll gladly update my review to reflect a more positive experience.

---‐-----------------------‐‐-----------------------------------------------
This is a legitimate review from a real client. Unfortunately, my experience with Justin Heideman and his firm reflects all of the concerns shared in other negative reviews—and in my opinion, they are entirely accurate. Justin made strong assurances but failed to follow through. The focus seemed to be more on generating bills than delivering results, and I believe their billing practices are ethically questionable. I would encourage anyone reading this to approach overly positive reviews with caution, as they may not reflect genuine client experiences. I deeply regret not trusting my initial instincts and seeking different legal counsel.

To Justin: Instead of spending time trying to identify which client left this review, I suggest focusing on honoring your commitments and following through on what you tell your clients.
