reviews.text: BE WARNED! I needed an attorney to protect my interests in the sale of my mineral rights. When I first spoke with Justin, I was excited because I thought he was intelligent, articulate and quick on his feet. Little did I realize he's also extremely arrogant, lazy (doesn't want to take the time to do anything right) and dishonest. He actually sent me a contract he told me to sign and return that he hadn't even read! When I realized there was not one single redline or change, I pressed him to see if he'd even looked it over. At first, he texted back yes. Then when I kept pushing because I could tell he hadn't, he backed off that answer and said he'd only checked at the deal points and they "looked inline." THEN, when I reacted with outrage because I'd nearly signed a document at the advice of counsel that contained added language that wasn't in the previous contract and would've committed me to more than what I'd agreed to do, he essentially admitted he hadn't read it at all but that I should "vet it" myself since I knew the deal better than he did. What? Are you kidding me? Vetting the contract was what I was paying him to do! After I fired him, I also had to hound him to get the balance of my retainer, and he made sure he took almost all of it, even though he could not have spent the time he claimed he did. STAY AWAY FROM THIS LOSER! He will only make your situation worse AND cost you a lot of money.
reviews.ownerResponse:
It is unfortunate each and every time any client leaves our firm in a dissatisfied fashion. It is more tragic, however, when the client elects to depart and then chooses to engage in personal attacks. Heideman & Associates is zealously committed to providing the highest quality legal advice and services. In this instance, a client issued specific instructions limiting the work Mr. Heideman was to perform so as to limit the expense the client would incur. The client also gave specific instructions on what "needed to be reviewed" because the client had already reviewed the documents, knew the terms, and simply wanted an attorney for the negotiations of the offers of resolution. The client then gave additional, specific, instructions regarding an offer of settlement and resolution. Mr. Heideman followed those instructions exactly and issued the precise offer of resolution the client ordered. The opposite party accepted the offer as stated to Mr. Heideman by the client. Mr. Heideman relayed this acceptance to the client. In response, the client expressed appreciation and gratitude. Subsequently, the client gave Mr. Heideman additional instructions. Mr. Heideman deemed these instructions inappropriate. As a result, Mr. Heideman refused to follow those instructions.

At this point, the client became angry, and attacked Mr. Heideman verbally - as illustrated in the client's review. Anyone reviewing this epithet-filled review is welcome to judge for themselves the level of professionalism demonstrated. However, as a law firm, the facts of this matter were specifically reviewed by multiple attorneys and staff, Mr. Heideman's refusal was also reviewed, and that independent evaluation determined that Mr. Heideman acted appropriately by refusing the instructions given. The client terminated the relationship when Mr. Heideman confirmed his refusal to engage in the conduct requested. The client was then free to engage subsequent counsel. Additionally, the firm issued a refund of the unearned portion of the client's retainer fee, and let the matter end. The Client has elected not to do so as demonstrated by this review. That is also unfortunate. Heideman & Associates wishes this client success in her future endeavors and hopes that she is able to find the legal representation she is more comfortable with.

I recently went through a DUI case along with a few additional charges, and working with Matt was one of the best decisions I could have made. reviews.ownerResponse: The outcome of this case was unfortunate, but had nothing to do with any lack of knowledge or preparation. Matt was hired to try to salvage this case after the client had 1. already plead guilty to a felony charge and 2. acknowledged in court that he had violated his probation. Matt spent almost three months gathering evidence, appearing at several hearings, and arguing on the client’s behalf. Unfortunately, the judge decided he wasn't willing to give the client another chance. Matt strongly disagreed with the judge’s decision and has nothing but sympathy for your son, Chris. Ultimately, the result in this case came down to the judge’s discretion.

reviews.ownerResponse: This claim is misleading and spurious and ignores the fact that the client hired Matt for two previous cases and was totally happy with the results. Yes, sometimes cases take a long time, and yes, in this case we filed—and the judge granted—a Motion to Withdraw. This was because of a conflict of interest, as required by ethical rules for lawyers. Not only was no trial set, but we arranged for ample time for the client to obtain other counsel, all of which was discussed with and approved by him prior to our withdrawal. Matt’s sandals were because of a temporary issue with an injured toe.

reviews.text:
At other firms, I had a very frustrating experience.  My original patent application was started by an attorney at a large IP firm.  That attorney quit to work for the USTPO, so I got a new attorney assigned to my case.  Just after I felt that he was up to speed he transferred to another firm and I decided to keep my relationship with him and so I found myself working with a new firm, then that attorney moved on and I got assigned to another who ended up moving to an out-of-state firm.  I decided to keep my relationship with that attorney, so I switched firms again.  When that attorney took an extended leave of absence, I had finally had enough.  That’s when I set out to find a small firm with experienced attorneys who were partners rather than employees.  After conducting a state-wide search, I found Goff IP Law and decided to give them a try.  Boy, am I glad I did.

Jared got up to speed in my application quickly and handled everything to get my case files transferred from my prior firm.  He is fantastic at keeping me informed about what’s happening in the process.  One thing in common with all my prior attorneys was that although they would notify me in a timely manner when we heard from the patent office, they would put off working the reply until just before the filing deadline.  It was common that by the time I got a draft of the response, there remained only a few days until the response was due, so I always felt rushed to approve their work.

Working with Goff IP Law has been different.  Jared has always gotten responses ready with plenty of lead time and had been very available to answer my questions and help me understand the process.  Moreover, the work has been first-quality work.  For example, when my application received an office action declining the invention’s patentability, Jared explained why he thought that the examiner was wrong and recommended that we file a written appeal brief to appeal the examiner’s decision to the appeal board.  The appeal brief was so well done that the examiner simply allowed the patent instead of responding to the brief.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for this review. It has been a pleasure working with you.

reviews.text:
Jared is an amazing attorney. We have worked together for several years now and I am always surprised/impressed with his ability to argue and get his claims past the examiners.
I have also been very impressed with Jared's quick response times and willingness to help us solve urgent problems. For example, a competitor went to our biggest customer falsely claiming that our products infringed their patents. This resulted in our customer putting orders on hold. Jared quickly jumped into action writing letters and trying to resolve the situation. When it was clear that the competitor would not stop, Jared put us in touch with an amazing legal group that ultimately helped us take this competitor to court. It was a painful experience but in the end everything worked out in our favor. We couldn't have done it without Jared's help. I highly recommend Goff IP!

reviews.text:
Johnson Livingston resolved two Underinsured Motorist claims that had grown too procedurally complex for me to advance with prior representation.

Both cases involved overlapping injury timelines from two separate accidents and treatment records spread across multiple providers. Todd Livingston took the first call, identified the specific coverages the cases required, and explained the order of available recovery clearly enough that I understood what the policies still supported. Brooke Hansen took primary responsibility for the legal strategy from that point, learning the medical details of both cases and building the demand around the documentation that established the full extent of recovery available.

Paralegal Minerva Morales coordinated records from every provider involved, resolved the billing discrepancies that had complicated the demand, and called monthly without my having to initiate contact. The team brought the same level of preparation to every stage of the process, and I would retain Johnson Livingston without hesitation for any matter of comparable complexity.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you so much for your 5-star review! We appreciate the feedback regarding your experience with our firm.

From the very beginning, it was clear that their top priority was caring for my dad and doing everything they could to help. They took on his case wholeheartedly, even knowing that they might not see any financial return. That spoke volumes about their character and compassion.

What stood out most was the way they fought for him with such dedication, even after he passed away. When something fell outside of their expertise, they didn’t hesitate to seek out the right people who could help—they built a team around my dad’s needs to ensure nothing was overlooked. Their communication throughout the entire process was phenomenal. Anytime I had a question or concern, no matter how big or small, they were there with patience and understanding.

This was the hardest time of my life, and while nothing could take that pain away, Blake, Todd, Emily, and the rest of their team lifted so much off my shoulders. They made the process a little easier, and for that I will always be grateful. They are not only excellent attorneys but truly compassionate people who go above and beyond for their clients.

reviews.text: Unprofessional, lets himself get pushed around, does NOT stand up for you or for the truth. If you communicate to him how you feel things are going with his "services" and improvements you would like to see, he is quick to dismiss your concerns, act in a rude manner by calling you a name and/or make false assumptions about you based on no evidence. He doesn't like getting constructive criticism. Trent only cares how the opposing counsel views him and wants to be accepted as part of the club. Therefore he has a tendency to let opposing counsel push him around and in effect join in with them in helping them with their case and being against his own client. Trent also gave terrible counsel or advice because he just didn't want to work hard and do what is right. Trent wants the low hanging fruit, doesn't want to rock the boat and actually stand up for his clients.

I would recommend Trent Cahill if you want to spend a lot of money for subpar performance and results. I would not recommend Trent Cahill if you want to be strongly represented.

I contacted Scott for a consultation after a very complicated motion to enforce in my case had gone sideways with a previous attorney making lots of mistakes. I was left picking up the pieces and representing myself, so I was looking for someone experienced and realistic about whether they could step in.

Scott was extremely upfront and honest from the start. He explained that he already had a heavy caseload and that my matter was more complex than what he had time for at the moment. I actually gained a lot of respect for him for that. Instead of overpromising or trying to “wing it,” he gave a straightforward assessment and didn’t waste my time.

He also provided recommendations for me after I had asked for them and while I wasn’t able to hire him, I would absolutely recommend him based on him being straightforward with me and saying how things are.  I’d much rather have that than someone who leads you on or pretends they have the time to handle something they can’t like my last lawyer did.
Attorney Scott Weight gets the job done! He is tough, blunt, faithful to your cause, and affordable.

Scott, and his team are the best there are. They are very thorough, quick to respond, detailed,  honest, and they explain every step along the way. Used a child's Autism against him when the child came forward about abuse. I sat in a case Scott was working on where a family was deciding custody for an Autistic child. The child claimed abuse from a step parent and did his best to communicate it to trusted adults despite being Autistic. I watched Scott Weight use the child's Autism against him in court claiming he was just anxious and probably made it up despite clear evidence of therapy the child had gone through and a restraining order that stated abuse had taken place. Scott made the focus of the case to disprove the child's claims and not focus on his safety during custody. Completely unethical to watch from any attorney but especially in the case of a young child on the Spectrum. Devastating to see him fight in court to disprove the child's experience instead of focus on the child's safety when figuring out custody. If you have children on the Spectrum or are on the spectrum yourself, I encourage you to find a different attorney to represent you.

reviews.text:
Unfortunately, my experience with Scott Weight was very discouraging, disappointing and emotionally damaging.

I waited to post this review until my divorce was finalized with a different attorney at another law firm. I felt it was important to go through the full process and gain a better understanding of what a healthy, professional lawyer-client relationship should look like—especially during something as difficult and emotional as a divorce. Now that I’ve experienced what real support and advocacy looks like, I feel it’s important to share my experience with Scott Weight.

As a client going through my only divorce and obviously not knowing much of anything about the process of divorce, I relied on him to guide me and help me feel prepared—especially for mediation. Instead, I was left completely unprepared for a very critical moment in the case. When mediation didn’t go well, rather than offering support or taking responsibility, he very harshly blamed me and was extremely disrespectful, even raising his voice while he lectured me on how it was my fault that I was so unprepared for mediation.

I was criticized and belittled during one of the most vulnerable and painful times of my life. Rather than easing the stress of the process, his behavior made everything more overwhelming, which raised my anxiety quite a bit and caused quite a bit more suffering. A divorce attorney should be an advocate and a source of support—not someone who adds to the emotional burden and blames their client for their own mistakes. It seemed to me that he simply didn’t want to do the work it takes to help his client be prepared for mediation. I am disabled and unable to work more than 15 hours a week and in the end Scott charged me thousands of dollars, which I definitely can’t afford and he had only made my life more difficult rather than trying to do right by me in any way. In my opinion, what he did to me was completely unethical. I definitely would not recommend anyone work with Scott Weight based on my traumatic and expensive experience.
It is very unfortunate that he was able to get away with this kind of treatment and still force me to pay him in the end.

I don’t leave reviews lightly, but Scott Weight and the team at Esplin & Weight deserve every bit of this five-star recognition. Regardless of the outcome, I had already made up my mind to write this review — because this firm fought tooth and nail for me in every way I asked them to.

While I can’t speak for every case they take on, I can speak for mine — and from start to finish, I never once doubted my decision to retain Scott and his team. They never sugarcoated my situation or fed me false hope. What I got was transparency, integrity, and the kind of fierce advocacy you want on your side when things get real.

If you choose to retain this firm, expect to have a warrior in your corner. I did, and I’ll never forget it. Should I ever need legal assistance again within their scope, there will be no hesitation — it will be Esplin & Weight, every time.

Thank you, Scott, and thank you to your team — from the bottom of my heart.

I hired Scott Weight as an attorney for my divorce case. He promised me he would work hard and get my case to trial as fast as possible when I retained him. He told me I could cancel my ex’s cell phone contact after temporary orders so I did and I was held in contempt which cost me 10k.  He made claims that he could download her phone and prove her infidelity, when he couldn’t. He added cost and time to my case by trying to find ways to close my case.  My case was already started by another firm that didn’t want to go to trial. When I hired him we where still in discovery and he didn’t do anything to prepare for trial except do a deposition  he just let all my time lapse. The deposition went long and he said it was to hard for him so he didn’t want to do anymore when we could have done 3 more hours.  He never scheduled trial within 12 months while he was my attorney, he failed to return emails and phone calls. When he didn’t understand what I wanted him to do for me he would threaten to drop me as a client. He told me he was to busy to work on my case and that I should find somebody else. No attorney goes to heaven.
reviews.ownerResponse: Some former clients unreasonably blame prior attorneys, many months after withdrawing from representing them, for their failure to obey court orders which the attorney would have no control over. Findings and orders detailing a person’s contempt of court are public record and if the truth is desired to be understood the reader should seek to obtain the public record in the court file to verify or dispel this former client’s claim. Mr. Weight disagrees entirely with this former client’s review.

reviews.text: Scott Weight is the best lawyer! He has been  amazing to work with. He is very knowledgeable and really cares about you. I wish I could fully convey my thoughts about Scott Weight. Scott took on my case midway through the process when my previous attorney quit my divorce. I found myself in a really bad spot having to find a new attorney that was willing to go to trial with a judge that was not in my favor and against my ex’s attorney who was free. Scott had good graces knowing that I came from an attorney that honestly loathed him, and he took up my banner in the middle of his heavy case load. I cannot tell you how indebted to him and his team for making the impossible possible.

By the time my trial came my case had been completely revamped. His partner Mckail worked diligently on my exhibits for my case and spent the time to really prepare all my witnesses before trial. We walked in to court with a better case than I had in the last year, and Scott’s acumen in the trial was shrewd and well informed. His arguments consistently with approval of the judge, and he deftly handled my ex’s attorney.

In the end I got what I fought for two years for, my kids and the equal right to be involved in their lives. My children and I are the direct benefactors of Scott’s skills and help. I absolutely give my recommendation to Scott as an attorney worth working with.

Scott Weight has been wonderful to work with! I had met with multiple attorneys prior to sitting down with Scott. I am relying on her experience and expertise to get me through this process, get the alimony needed and custody that's right for my situation. She has had a lot of experience with divorce law. The office has been very nice to work with. I hope it continues to go well and is over soon.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for the review.

reviews.text: I worked with Lorie Fowlke. I think she is very knowledgeable about the law and I felt that my case was pretty simple and straightforward and my ex-husband and I had settled everything except the amount of alimony that he would be paying. However, it took 2 years and many thousands of dollars to complete. Some of it was due to a change in attorneys on my ex-husbands side but I was very frustrated about the length of time it took to resolve this and the amount of money that it took. Lorie was great about calming me down when I would get frustrated and she was very ethical about the way things were handled.  My only complaint was that she had went through several staff changes and my paperwork that I brought to her in the beginning went through several hands and some of it was not went through or I would have to get copies of things I had already brought in because Lorie or her staff couldn't locate them. I felt like I had to continue to go over the same things and find things that had to do with my case because she didn't seem like she knew my case.  However, she does know the law and has been very kind and straightforward about all my concerns and questions. My divorce case ended up being settled in mediation. There were many things that caused delays in this case but they were not because of me. I didn't feel like I got the information that I needed to be kept up to speed with what was going on.  I would have to call or e-mail or make an appointment to go in and talk to her to find out what was happening in my case and I feel like thats where a lot of the charges on my bill were because we kept having to go over the same things. In fairness, I must say that I have not been involved with attorneys very much so I don't really have a baseline to compare my experience with others. I feel like my ex-husband got away with a lot more than he should have because Lorie would tell me that he couldn't do some of the things he was doing but when I would try to get her to act on the things he was doing that were illegal, she would just tell me that it would take more of my time and money to rectify what he was doing. So, I think my ex-husband got away with a lot of stuff that he perhaps wouldn't of with a more aggressive attorney.
Dart will not call me! He is definitely overworked by the courts.

Kyle Dart is the worst attorney we have ever worked with. Disgusting business practices, will NOT give you an itemized statement of his “services”, will “bill” you for over a thousand and do the BARE MINIMUM, very sketchy and unprofessional. Beware, he will go weeks and even MONTHS without responding to important emails, because of this I didn’t get to see my own son for over 2 1/2 years due to Kyle’s PURE NEGLIGENCE on my case as well as my fiancé’s! Will make false promises about getting things done, horrible communication, and will leave you on read and even BLOCK YOUR NUMBER WHEN YOU NEED HELP THE MOST. Because of Kyle, it has been better to go through this nightmare PRO BONO. Working with him was nothing but a headache, and firing him was the best decision we ever made. We are beyond frustrated and disgusted. My best advice if you’re considering to hire Kyle is to RUN AS FAST AS YOU CAN!
I just want to say that Aaron was very wonderful to work with and very prompt to get back with me and settle my case five stars all around thank you again

I did not follow through with my bankruptcy. The reason I did not, is that after gathering all my financial records, from my income, my expenses, my debts etc, and presenting them to the legal team of Lincoln Law, they told me the honest truth. They told me that if I did want to pursue my case for bankruptcy, it was the longest of long shots that this would be approved by a judge.
It was one of their representatives, Dawn, who went into good detail about how much money I make, and how to use my finances efficiently in order to pay off my finance, save my excessive income, and build my credit score to a healthy rate once again. She told me honestly and kindly that if I did pursue this case of bankruptcy, it would harm my credit score indefinitely, and it would hinder my chances of employment, housing, future loans, and so forth.
Dawn and the rest of the team here were very thorough, very articulate, and very kind with their services. Though it isn't what I thought it would be, the service I received was incredibly well. I of course recommend this team for anyone else seeking bankruptcy or to see if bankruptcy is warranted.

reviews.text:
I am an octogenarian. I was in need of professional estate planning help. After reviewing online listings for local estate planning attorneys I was drawn to Legacy Bridge Legal primarily because of the biographical information for Marne Pehrson, attorney and founder. I was impressed by the fact that she has had experience dealing with older individuals in non legal matters, prior to becoming an attorney, and felt that this would be helpful in understanding my needs.
Upon meeting Marne, I noted that she is a warm and friendly young woman who immediately made me feel comfortable discussing my estate planning goals and needs with her. She is knowledgeable and carefully answered my questions and explained estate planning matters that I was unfamiliar with.
I am completely satisfied with the very professional results of the estate planning services rendered by Marne Pehrson and Legacy Bridge Legal and I am happy to give a Five Star recommendation.
Richard Johns
reviews.ownerResponse: Richard, your kind words mean so much to me! It has been a pleasure working with you and helping you navigate your estate planning needs. reviews.text: We had procrastinated a long time about estate planning, but a compelling postcard advertisement from Legacy Bridge Legal finally galvanized us into action. She also knew  how to answer our questions and assuage our doubts, and how to be patient with our struggles with decision-making. Going through the immigration process is stressful, emotional, and honestly overwhelming. I was scared and unsure of what to expect, but from the moment I spoke with M. Paige Benjamin, I felt like I had someone truly on my side.

He didn’t just treat me like a case number — he treated me like a real person, with respect, compassion, and patience. Every time I had a question (and I had a lot!), Paige Benjamin took the time to explain things in a way I could actually understand. He never rushed me or made me feel like a burden, and that meant more than I can say.

There were moments I felt hopeless or anxious, but Paige Benjamin always kept me grounded and focused. He guided me through every form, every step, and every challenge. I could feel how much he cared — not just about the legal process, but about my life and my future.

Thanks to his hard work and dedication, my case was approved, and now I can finally breathe again. I’ll never forget how supported I felt, and I would recommend Paige Benjamin to anyone who needs help with immigration. You won't just get a great lawyer — you’ll get someone who actually cares.

reviews.text:
If you are on the fence about whether or not you should hire an attorney, do it. The final settlement is far greater with an attorney and more than compensates for any attorney fees. Plus there is little to no work on your part. Now that you have decided to hire an attorney, Young and Young Law is who I would recommend. We had the unfortunate need to hire an attorney twice. Once was for our parents who were injured in an auto accident. Tyler Young dealt with all the hospital inquiries, Workmans Comp, insurance and everything in between and got us the best settlement we could have hoped for. The second time was more of a referral for an "Aunt". This one went to trial and after all of the low ball offers and attempts to show our aunt was not seriously injured, again the final settlement was more than we could hope for. After both cases all medical bills were satisfied and both parties ended up with a large sum of money to help with any further issues that may arise and that was after any and all attorney fees. Do yourself a favor. If you are in need of an attorney, see Young and Young Law.
Sincerely,
Troy and Suzy Chapman

Though I worked for them, I didn’t know what it was like to go through a personal injury myself. Now as Tyler and Allen’s client, they strengthened me through this personal injury and brought me a peace of mind I didn’t know was possible.

If you’re in a situation such as personal injury, wrongful death, car accident, etc, I am sorry! I know with Tyler and Allen you will feel MUCH less overwhelmed and you will be able to focus on your needs. Because Allen and Tyler lifted the stress load from me, I was able to focus more on the therapies I needed which helped me heal faster and better. I’m sure that it’d be the same for you! reviews.text:
I would give Zabrieskie Law Firm zero stars if I could. This law firm demonstrated a complete lack of care for their clients and exhibited serious negligence in handling our case.

We initially hired them for a criminal trial, and they negotiated an abeyance agreement. We complied with all the terms of the abeyance. However, at the end of the abeyance period, the firm failed to take the necessary steps to have it closed out, despite claiming that this was covered under the initial retainer. They did not contact us at all during the abeyance period.

Ten months later, we received an email informing us that an order to show cause had been issued because the abeyance was never closed. We were told we were now in violation of the original agreement – a situation that would not have occurred if the firm had fulfilled its initial obligation. They then demanded an additional $1,000 retainer to represent us in this new matter, which was a direct result of their negligence.

It took five months of persistent communication attempts to get the firm to take any action on the case. Furthermore, during this period, secretaries and paralegals made unprofessional and damaging statements to us, claiming we were guilty and citing inadmissible evidence. This behavior was completely inappropriate and undermined any sense of trust in their representation.

Communication with this firm was consistently poor. They appear to take on too many clients, resulting in delayed responses and a lack of clear explanations. They also seemed to lack proactive communication with the prosecutor, hindering progress on our case. We frequently had to "bug" them to get them to do anything. When we attempted to follow up, they accused us of being annoying and blocked our calls and texts. This is unacceptable behavior when clients are facing the stress and anxiety of criminal charges and are paying for representation.

Zabrieskie Law Firm failed to make us a priority and did not provide the support and reassurance we expected from our legal representation. I strongly advise against hiring this firm. Seek representation elsewhere, where you will be treated as a priority and receive competent and attentive service, instead of being exploited for money.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for taking the time to share your experience with us. We sincerely apologize for any frustration and disappointment you faced. At Zabriskie Law Firm, we strive to provide all our clients with attentive and diligent representation, and we regret that you felt otherwise during your interactions with us. Your feedback is invaluable and will be used to improve our services and communication practices. We would appreciate the opportunity to discuss your concerns further and invite you to reach out to us directly so we can look into your case in detail and find a satisfactory resolution.

reviews.text: I was pulled over and sent to jail for a DUI after blowing a zero alcohol level and passing a urine test administered by a nurse from the Provo police station. You can find my review with the Provo police station under my name. I decided to hire a lawyer because I was scared that I could get charged with a felony and sent to jail when I didn’t commit a crime. I never told the police that I was guilty their excuse for taking me in was that their could be something in my system and it’s not showing. I had to call my lawyer when I went to court because he was not there. I spoke to the secretary and she told me that I didn’t have court while I was calling them from the Provo court house. She then said she would let my lawyer know. Then my lawyer showed up and went in a room to discuss my case with the prosecutors. My blood results had came back and were also showing nothing. The lawyer was so happy for me that he could get me off with just me taking some AA classes.. and I wouldn’t be charged with anything. Why would I need to take classes when I had nothing in my system. I ended up spending around $4000 dollars from getting back my car from the tow yard to the lawyer and then the classes. I hired my lawyer expecting him to fight for me and ask why was I even taken to jail that night when I was never impaired. Zabriskie definitely gave me a low price however as the saying goes you get what you paid for. I myself am going to speak to the Provo police and try to get some reimbursement unless Zabriskie wants to try and really want to help me out themselves. I recently went to get insurance and the DUI shows up for them when running my name. So even though I was never charged it still affecting my life. I want Zabriskie to reach out to me so we can resolve my case in the right way. If not I have no problem myself in contacting the Provo Police and working on it myself.
reviews.ownerResponse: We genuinely apologize for the distress you experienced. Our top priority is always to assist our clients effectively. Please contact our office directly so that we can address your concerns and explore ways to resolve any issues. Thank you for your feedback.

reviews.text:
I want to start off by saying that I am truly skeptical of all these "5 star reviews". They seem tailored and paid for.

These lawyers did NOT represent the way they said they would over the phone. Once they were paid, not a word was heard from them for over a year. The case kept getting passed over to different lawyers and requests for info were ignored or completely dismissed. There was NO attempt whatsoever to even build a defense.

Paid them to act as a middle man between a third party and the prosecutor. During attempts to build a defense they had rejected it and even said "Well, we have a certain relationship to maintain with judges and prosecutors in this town".

Dean showed up to court late, laughed at the situation and said "that's lawyers for ya".  You're definitely better off with a public defender.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for sharing your experience with us, Kenna. We are deeply sorry to learn about your dissatisfaction and any inconvenience you encountered. We strive for excellence in serving our clients and take such feedback seriously. Our client relationships and case management are utmost priorities. We would like to understand what happened and how we can make things right. Please contact our office directly at your earliest convenience so we can discuss your case further and address your concerns. Thank you for bringing this to our attention.

I had an excellent experience with Fillmore Spencer after an accident. From the outset, the team demonstrated a high level of professionalism, legal expertise, and commitment to my case.

They provided constant support, clearly explained each stage of the process, and diligently and effectively defended my rights. Thanks to their experience and personalized attention, I was able to face a difficult situation with confidence and peace of mind.

I highly recommend Fillmore Spencer for their professional ethics, efficiency, and compassionate approach.
Excellent service, very grateful for the results.

Fillmore Spencer did a horrible job, cost me money, and ensured that I will remain in pain because of their lack of diligence.  I was working with Randy Spencer on a car accident case that we just finalized from 3 years ago.  I did almost 2 years of physical therapy, doctors appointments, Injections, Chiropractic work, and decompression.  I am still in horrific pain, but my doctor told me to stop treatment and look into alternative care options because the treatments we were doing were not helping, but hurting.  I found a clinic that does injections and electrical pulse therapy to strengthen my back muscles again which is what I need done.  The clinic does not operate with insurance liens, so I was told by my legal representation to get a future care treatment plan from the clinic and that would be figured into the case and I would be awarded the money needed to get my therapy.  I had spoke with the legal office several times about what my treatment plan was, and they were told all of the details.  They get an offer from the insurance to settle without even considering the additional care cost of around $150,000, and I wanted to go through arbitration to try to get the cost of the future care covered.  This is when Randy Spencer called me on the phone to convince me not to go through arbitration because I would most likely end up with less money because they will not view the treatment plan as a legitimate treatment even though I have had a few treatments done that I paid for out of pocket, and they worked for me.  I asked why they let me go through all of the work of going through the trouble of getting a future treatment plan that the insurance would just disregard, and I still have not gotten an answer on that.  So basically, They took 1/3 of my settlement, but my "Legal Counsel" didn't "Counsel" me.  They ended up just costing me money.  There was no reason for them to know what my treatment plan was for a year, wait until its too late to find a different treatment plan, then just tell me that treatment plan would be viewed as Taboo.  I gave them around $33,000 of my settlement amount and they did not guide me correctly through this.  I will never use this attorneys office again, and I hope nobody else ends up throwing away money on such negligence.

Randy Spencer was a huge help during a stressful time. Our insurance company wasn’t willing to cover us after an accident, and Randy stepped in quickly. He was very responsive, asked the right questions, and kept things moving. He sent a strong letter to the insurer with citations to Utah law and made it clear he was prepared to file a complaint. Almost immediately after that, the insurance company changed their position and covered us.

Randy was efficient and mindful about keeping his billable hours down, and he genuinely focused on making sure we were treated fairly. I’m very grateful for his help and would recommend him without hesitation.
reviews.ownerResponse: Doug--thank you for your kind review. It was a pleasure to assist you and persuade the insurance company to treat you right!

Well, this game comes to my mind when I think about lawyer Mark Dodd. I had an incident where I really thought I had a case and contacted many different lawyers in Utah. Mark was the only one out of all of them who wanted to see the paperwork and really review it. For free, he took the time and pondered on my case. I know he’s a busy guy, and I am so appreciative that he would do that for me. Ultimately, he said, I did not have a case, but explained to me why.  I told him that  was the first time that I had heard any of “the reasons” and was so impressed by him. He’s just a great human and a great lawyer. Thank you Mark. I appreciate you even if it didn’t turn out to be the answer that I wanted, you made me feel right about not going any further with my case. If ever I need someone like you in the future, you’ll be the first one I’ll be calling!

I have been working with Fillmore Spencer for over a year with Mark Dobbs the first couple months I made sure I got all the paperwork they needed on time within a week of being asked about it I spent the next year doing tests and stuff with different doctors each time I had to get an appointment it took months so that  is why it is taking all this time he had to get one paper and took him over 3 months I've been contacted twice in the past month about Him or is Aid getting ahold of me neither time high have I been called or contacted there's been at least five times he has not contacted me when he set up appointments every time I call him he's busy I never get a call back I feel like the 33.5% he is getting I'm throwing down the garbage because I have done all the work up to now to get anything done I really would like to know what he's getting paid for I guess nothing because he has done nothing he has contacted workman's comp at least to get my appointments for the doctors but that's all I know he is done his aides have done everything else but still it shouldn't have taken months to make a phone call to workman's comp to get my appointment I'm sorry about the one star but something really needs to get done

Terrible place! My mother hired Adam to help us with a divorce case, paid him a retainer which he stated would be most of the case and probably wouldn't cost much more. It was usually difficult to get ahold of him, about 8 months into the case I was told my mother owes an additional $7,000 and then more lost communication and my mother lost her court case and everything she owns!
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. I am truly sorry to hear about your disappointment with the outcome of your mother’s case. I understand how emotional and difficult these matters can be, and I know that the result was not what your family had hoped for.

This was ultimately a fact-specific legal issue, and despite presenting all the evidence and witnesses available to us, the judge ruled against your mother based on the statute.

With respect to communication and billing, we met multiple times in person, by phone, and through email and text during the course of the case. From the outset, I explained that the retainer would be applied toward fees and that additional costs could arise depending on how the case developed, this is also reflected in the engagement agreement. In fact, when the initial retainer was exhausted, we worked out a very minimal payment plan in order to continue representing your mother, and I invested significant time and work even after funds were depleted.

I know it is frustrating when a case does not end in the desired result, but I want to assure you that we worked diligently to present the strongest case possible based on the facts as they were given to us. I wish your family the very best moving forward. -- Adam

reviews.text: Debbie the paralegal,would get the lions share of cudos,& credit,& I'm sure did the lions share of all of the wrk also.In all honesty, I didn't relate to the atrny very well named Randy,especially to start with,& when I came to the firm per recommendation of my kind of a prestigious Relative of mine,I knew nothing of the firm,& when I asked a few questions to start with,Randy seemed to act kind of insulted,like I was doubting his reputation,or integrity or sumthing,& such,when I knew absolutely nothing about him or his staff,My Relative knew the owner,& he passed it down to Randy apparently.In all fairness,he did give me I believe 45 days to do it for a lesser amnt,@ 10% I think vrs the 33 & 1/3rd they normally charge,by me doing all of the leg work,& such,but didn't receive much help & counsel,on sum questions I had.Randy did score points with me by saying he wished he knew just how much money these companies made by taking their time,& getting interest on things,& saving money to the last minute to be sent,& now in retrospect,I wish I would have done it in two separate parts,Which we did in essence still,but only signd up for the first part of the case to beat a deadline to start with,& then consider the second part of the under insurance separate,& not all run into one from the start.Anyway,Im glad it's over now,& for any efforts that wer made in my behalf,Especially by Debbie.Thankyou Sincerely.Ronald Stanley Bateman

If there were an option for less than one star, it would be more appropriate. Craig is, without a doubt, the most unprofessional, incompetent attorney I have ever seen. You will be belittled, made fun of, condemned, and berated all throughout your time with this company. They gaslight you and make you feel so guilty for ending up in the situation that you did. The only thing these men care about is their precious egos.

reviews.text: Mary was the best in representing my case she was down to the point and didn’t hesitate to call or text me. She was very responsive with any questions or concerns I had.

When my family member suddenly needed legal help I thought I could handle it myself.  A friend suggested getting an attorney. Luckily I took the advice. I told her what I needed and she checked all the boxes-she knew and had good relations with the prosecutor, she knew the judge and she specialized in this kind of case.
At crunch time she did everything I had hired her for.  She is a skilled negotiator and has the respect of the court.  I can unequivocally recommend her to anyone who finds themselves in need of top rate represention.

reviews.text: I have no words to thank lawyer Mary enough for her outstanding work. I would recommend her without hesitation. A thousand thanks to her and to God for bringing her into my life. Thank you so much!

I honestly don’t know where I’d be right now if it weren’t for Mary Martinez and the team at Martinez Law Firm

Mary is not just an incredibly sharp and knowledgeable attorney — she’s also one of the most compassionate people I’ve ever met. I was facing a serious third-degree felony charge that could have turned my entire life upside down. From the very first consultation, Mary listened without judgment, explained every step clearly, and fought for me like she truly cared — because she did.

Throughout the process, Mary was steady, confident, and incredibly thorough. She explained every step in plain language, prepared me for everything, and always made time when I had questions or just needed reassurance. She saw through the noise and helped prove the truth — that I was innocent.

I can’t thank her enough, and I hope this review helps someone else find the help they deserve.

At a completely unexpected moment, I found myself caught up in a very serious legal issue—one that shook not only my emotional stability but also deeply affected my entire family. It felt overwhelming and nearly impossible to deal with… until I decided to hire Attorney Mary Easton.

From the very beginning, she gave me the peace of mind I so desperately needed. Her confidence, experience, and deep knowledge of the law turned what felt like an impossible situation into something manageable. She didn’t just handle my case—she guided me through it with clarity and strength, making a complex process feel less intimidating. Not because it was easy, but because she knew exactly what to do.

In the end, I came out of it all in the best possible way—essentially free of legal problems. I honestly don’t have words to fully express my gratitude.

If you’re facing legal trouble and reading this, take it as a sign. Don’t wait. Call Mary Easton. Whatever the issue, she’ll fight for you and give you the best shot at peace and justice.
Great service 👏 I highly recommend 👌

Mary is a tough negotiator and very detailed. For this I am so appreciative.
More importantly, she has a heart of gold and was so wonderful to work with, she is happy, upbeat and sweet which makes her better than G.O.A.T, she is a unicorn!

Forever grateful!!!!

I had a felony case and Mary picked it up and told me not to worry. She would let me know anytime something new came up and would reassure me that everything will be ok. Today she got my case dismissed! She is genuinely the best lawyer I’ve ever met thank you so much for everything!
reviews.ownerResponse: It was an absolute pleasure :)

reviews.text:
Working with Attorney Martinez has been the most rewarding experience I've ever had. She treated my case as her own, explained everything to me from the beginning, and the outcome couldn't have been better. I recommend her without hesitation; she is the best criminal lawyer in all of Utah.

Working with Attorney Martinez has been the most rewarding experience I've ever had. She treated my case as her own, explained everything to me from the beginning, and the result couldn't have been better. I recommend her without hesitation; she is the best criminal lawyer in all of Utah.

I reached out to many law firms over a felony charge before coming across The Martinez Law Firm,  Let me tell you all, Mary Martinez is an amazing attorney and someone I am now glad to be able to call a friend! I must’ve had 5-10 over the phone consultations and a couple in person with other law firms, not one of them put me at ease and talked to me about my case the way Mary did. With the other law firms I either felt pressured to choose them right away there on the spot or as if they were trying to instill fear in me to just retain them because they were “the best”. Not Mary, she talked to me as if we have known each other for years, was very humble and experienced she explained/walked me through my whole case over the phone was blunt and honest about how things could go and Went above and beyond to help me out, if I had any questions I could call/text her and she would always get back to me quickly whenever she was available. Even when she was busy she would message me back to tell me she will give me a call in a few. What a wonderful person this woman is, even before court hearings we can have a conversation about the case and share laughs.  I also have to give a shout out to her assistant Tessa! Wow another wonderful person at the Martinez law firm! She sent out reminders for my court dates, invoices, answered simple questions when Mary wasn’t available and handled all my paperwork that I needed to turn in for court. As long as I did my part these two wonderful ladies had my back and put my mind at ease! I would recommend Mary and her law firm to anyone that needs help!
The Fees for her services are very fair from all the other law firms I called. I definitely picked the correct law firm! Beyond happy with the outcome of my case and the new friend I’ve made.
reviews.ownerResponse: Hugo- always happy to help in anyway amigo :)

reviews.text:
I Albert was arrested on DUI on Aug 28th. After a few days I had a bunch of letters from Utah attorneys and I shown them to my General foreman, he read all the letters and he picked Mary Martinez Law Firm due to their description. I sent a text message and Mary called me the next day talked to me politely about my arrest and I narrated everything to her. She said 'Albert I can handle it' She contacted the court and postponed my court date from Sept 19th to Oct 26th while waiting for my blood sample.
During that time we kept on communication til today Oct 26th my court date and happily for me, the case was dismissed. I'm saying this to praise Mary Martinez Law Firm for a good job done and I appreciate it.
Thank you Mrs Mary Martinez.
reviews.ownerResponse: Albert you were such a pleasure to represent :) .

reviews.text: I want to thank Scott Mosley for taking on my case against a prominent University in a different state when so many other attorneys wouldn't touch it. Thank you for being fearless. I truly believe it was God and your representation that helped me remain in my school program. I am very satisfied with the outcome of the case. I'm proud to say I graduated this year. I finally have my Master's degree which this university tried to prevent me from obtaining with all of their might. Underdogs like myself get railroaded all the time, so I'm very appreciative and grateful for your talents in securing the victory.

My wife and I recently encountered a difficult real estate issue with building our home.   We asked around for referrals and Seiler, Anderson, Fife and Marshall came up several times from different individuals.  So I gave them a call.

I got in touch with Mr. Fife.   I was incredibly impressed with his professionalism.  He listened to my situation, asked questions, really applied to understand to situation and then after that offered some recommendations on what to do.  He also went through several different scenarios of what could happen and what to do in those situations.

We are most grateful for his help and he helped greatly relieved much of the anxiety we were feeling.    I would recommend him to anyone needing help with real estate transactions.

I have been consulting with Mr Fife at this firm regarding contract law at different times for over 3 years. Their customer service is outstanding and so is their integrity. His counsel has helped me and saved me from a bully contractor. I am not a business owner that is constantly giving them business yet they treat me as if I was one of their top clients. I would recommend them to my family and friends any time!

We were given background legal information that assisted us enormously in making informed decisions regarding the various aspects of the estate planning process. Extremely pleased and came away from the experience confident in our “plan”.

I contact Morgan fife who directed me to Lacee Whimpey who took my case and believed the other party had wronged me. She worked hard on getting the best settlement amount for me. I hope I will never need a lawyer again, but if I do, I will certainly be contacting this firm.

I can't recommend the firm of Seiler, Anderson, Fife, & Marshall enough. I have a personal relationship with Mr. Fife and know first hand that he is a man of great character and integrity. What I did not know was how excellent a lawyer he is or what an outstanding law firm he is a part of. Mr Fife represented me in small claims court and went above and beyond any expectation. He was well prepared, knowledgeable, and very professional. After he won my case the Judge took the time to compliment him on being so well prepared. The staff I worked with to handle billing was courteous and professional as well. At the time of judgment Mr. Fife was unable to collect the money owed and I was not sure, due to some unique circumstances, I would ever receive the money I was awarded. However, Mr. Fife  doggedly pursued the matter through several years and was able to collect the amount owed me with interest and fees! The firm paid me  promptly and I could not be more pleased. My case was small and the money was not very much so it could have been easy to just write me off, but Seiler, Anderson, Fife & Marshall is not that type of law firm. I mattered to them just as much as a large client and was treated with a high level of personal care and respect.  I highly recommend them for any type of legal need.

I always appreciate the way Morgan Fife works with me and my clients.  He approaches each person in a considerate, thoughtful and professional manner.  You can't help but feel his passion for helping people and wanting the best solution possible.

An amazing law firm, with attorneys covering a wide range of abilities. I have never had a bad experience with any of the attorneys there. I really appreciate the care they show and the confidence they give me. Clearly my first choice.
Morgan Fife is awesome. I would definitely recommend him for family and friends.

reviews.text: I am extremely disappointed with this lawyer. I came to her hoping for guidance and support, and instead, I was met with frustration and arrogance. When I tried to provide additional details about my situation—thinking it might help—she reacted as if I was bothering her. She curtly reminded me that she was the district representative and said that if I didn’t like it, I should find someone else. It was disheartening and unprofessional. I expected understanding and respect, but sadly, I received neither. I can’t recommend her to anyone.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for sharing your thoughts. I genuinely appreciate your comments and want to acknowledge your frustration. I’m truly sorry that our interaction left you feeling disappointed.

As much as I care about helping people, I have a professional and ethical obligation as an attorney not to provide legal advice to individuals who are not my clients. In this situation, you were exploring the possibility of applying for a position at the company I represent, but had not formally submitted an application. While I had limited authorization from my client to communicate with you, your questions eventually shifted into legal territory—something I am ethically prohibited from addressing without a formal attorney-client relationship.

All of our communication took place via email, and I understand that tone and intent can sometimes be misinterpreted in written messages. Please know it was never my intention to be dismissive or unhelpful. I recommended that you contact another attorney who could give your concerns the time, attention, and legal guidance they deserve.

I sincerely wish you the best as you move forward in finding the right support.

reviews.text: Kim was a beacon of clarity during a frustrating and confusing process. I initially tried to file our marriage green card application on my own and only realized later that I had submitted just one of several required forms. I was overwhelmed and worried I’d make serious mistakes. From the moment we started working with Kim, everything changed. She answered all our questions with patience, simplified the entire process, and brought us peace of mind. She even met with us on Christmas Eve to help finalize our packet so we could submit it ASAP. When we got our interview notice, she responded quickly—scheduling a late-night Zoom call to walk us through what to expect and gave us a detailed checklist to stay organized. Thanks to her guidance, we felt confident and prepared—and the interview went smoothly. Kim is not just a knowledgeable attorney, but a kind and supportive person who genuinely cares about her clients.  We’re so grateful for her help and can’t recommend her enough!

reviews.text:
I hired Kim for the first time in 2008 and I did it four more times after that.
She did a great job of making sure that the required documentation was turned in on time. I appreciated Kim being very honest about the chances of my case whether favorable or not.
It is important to note that I ended up doing a lot of the work of collecting my own information from my employer. Things that I presumed the firm should have done. I even had to write my own paper advertisements. I thought that was a little odd since they are supposed to be the experts about wording things right. Her personality is cold and she can come across as rude. I felt like she knew that my family needed her services specially because she was not very expensive so she was dismissive in most of her communication. One time, her secretary called me to reschedule an appointment twice. I was a little frustrated with that since I needed to take days off from work. I told that to the secretary. Kim called me the very same day and "reprimanded" me saying that I didn't have the right to make her secretary feel bad because she took really good care of her staff and she wasn't going to let me to offend her feelings. -Yeah! Kim called her client and "reprimanded" me for requesting more consideration in planning meeting with the client.

After I got my residency, her tone toward me turned to be more cordial, which I think it was interesting to say the least.

I would recommend Kim for anyone who is looking for a straight-forward answer and you want to know your honest possibilities, although don't expect great customer service.
reviews.ownerResponse: Hi Geo, I would love the opportunity to talk to see how we can improve. I’ll reach out to you on Monday or you are welcome to contact me on my cell or email me this weekend.

reviews.text: If you're looking for a defense attorney I highly recommend Petro and Aston Law to represent you. Their unwavering dedication and commitment to your personal needs is exemplary.
Number one law firm in the state hands down.. #1‐  Mr. Petro and his team are a beast, when it comes to defending his clients.
Mike and Leah are phenomenal, very experienced and educated. They are always willing to listen and help however they can.

If You’re Facing False Allegations, Stop Searching and Call Leah

When false allegations nearly ended my life as I knew it, Leah didn’t just take my case — she took it personally. She believed me when it felt like no one else did, fought like my freedom was her own, and tore apart every lie with surgical precision.

If you’re ever facing something like this, especially in a domestic case, call Leah. She’s relentless, she’s sharp, and she’s the one you want in your corner when everything’s on the line. She goes above and beyond for you as a person unlike anything I’ve experienced. Highly recommend.
Petro and Aston are super great Mike and Leah really helped me out and made things less stressful. Always get back to me to answer my questions. Alexia is super nice and helpful as well.

Leah easily ranks 10/10! I am greatful for her knowledge of the system, and ability to communicate the most essential matters to the court. She was able to protect the best interest of my children & ensure a positive outcome for my side. I feel absolutely blessed to have had her represent me.

Leah Ashton is by far the worst attorney that has ever represented me, absolutely horrible communication. On top of that she gave me horrible legal advice that has made me look bad with the courts. If you’re looking to hire this firm please reconsider. There is a lot of attorneys who will care, communicate, and fight for you more than this firm.

I would give Leah 100 stars if google would let me! The whole team was absolutely fantastic. I never planned on needing a defense attorney, but I am so grateful I found Leah to help me through the process. She listened, communicated, and walked me through the whole process. She answered all my questions and was always extremely attentive to my  needs. She offered great counsel and worked extremely hard for me. I could not be more thankful for all of her hard work! If I ever find myself in need of an attorney, she will absolutely be my first call!

With her profound grasp of the law, coupled with her strategic acumen, Leah Aston possesses impeccable timing. She pays the price to understand a case (my own was exceedingly complex and high profile). Ms. Aston has a sixth sense to develop its strengths and how to expose the prosecution’s weaknesses. In other words, Leah knows how to flip the script. I am pleased to give Leah my highest rating, both as an attorney and as a human being. Are 5 stars even enough when your life is on the line?

Pleasant, smart, and good attorneys. I was a little angry and frustrated at times due to the nature of my case and took it out on Leah/Jade inadvertently. They were kind and set me straight. I’d recommend them to friends and family.

I was innocent and he was all nice and telling me I had nothing to worry about then later on dropped me because I couldn’t understand why he wouldn’t talk to me or answer questions.  When I had my drivers license hearing he did almost nothing to help me keep my license.  He and his partner (who was very abrasive) wanted me to take a deal that was not a deal in any sense if the word.  I was grateful they dropped me because I got a public defender who was awesome, had no problem holding my hand and answering wwyesryibs, and who got me the best possible deal.  She would have fought for me in court if I had chosen that.  And she was free.  I think lawyers like Petro get your money then they want to take care of the problem the easiest way they can so they can get money from other defendants.  If you did nothing wrong he is not the lawyer for you.  If you are guilty and want a plea deal he’s probably for you although he doesn’t right for a better deal. I was disappointed because everyone told me he was the best around.  Not in my experience.

I hired Mike and Leah to help me with a DUI case. I also had some buddies of mine, get into the same trouble further down the road me and my buddies all had a great experience with Mike and his team they were Super responsive I was always aware of my court dates, fees payments, etc. thank you again

I used this office for two of my cases they were easy to work with super responsive and greatly appreciated to me today I recommend them to everyone I know, I think you should do the same, thanks again to Mike and his team
Impossible to get ahold of.
For years he's been a very loyal and reliable resource, and I could not recommend him enough. I would give him 10/10 stars if I could.

I hired Michael Petro and Leah to help me with a reckless driving charge. I was charged with 4 infractions and after Michael and Leah worked on my case they were able to get all the charges dropped and case dismissed. I walked away with nothing on my record. Hiring them was worth every penny!
Wouldn’t trust anyone else to handle my very complicated case. He’s highly recommended by many. Extremely amazing attorney!

Leah and Mike were fantastic! We had a unique case and they took it and helped us every step of the way, spending extra time they didn’t have to with us! We will forever be grateful for our experience with them. The outcome was just what we needed. Highly recommend them!
Mike and Leah did amazing work with a family member's case. We heard by word of mouth that they were the best in Utah County. Our family definitely agrees with that!
Did an excellent job while helping me through some stressful times. they were very communicative, fair and helpful throughout the whole process, would recommend 100%.
Thank you Leah for all your help with my case so much, it was very stressful for me!

I am extremely grateful for the two years of ongoing support and representation Mike Petro and associates provided me.
Knowing I was not the easiest of clientele during that time of stress and struggle,  Mike and his office got me through the whole ordeal in one peice.
As far as I knew, prices were fair and the services I received for the prices were beyond what any other attorney has ever provided.
Thank you all.

Definitely would not recommend. They were not ready for my court date twice and didn't communicate that to me so I missed two days of work for nothing. They had months to prepare both times. Now I have to deal with probation for 6 months longer than I should have. Absolutely not worth the money.
Amazing communication and delivered on everything I’d ever hope for. Very kinda and answer any questions I had. 10/10 recommend
Mike Petro went above and beyond in our case.
Great team, very helpful, and knowledgeable. I would confidently say that they care about their clients, which can be a hard thing to find. I would definitely recommend them.
Mike Petro is one of the best attorneys you will find. He is very smart, focuses on what's important, and does what he says. I would not hesitate to recommend him to family and friends.
Mike Petro is the BEST!!

Where do I begin..? I am so grateful I fell into the hands of Lea to work on my case. She was professional, real, straight to the point, and a sweet lady with an awesome accent!! The things she did for me and my case are mind boggling to the point where I even wonder how she did it. I am forever grateful toward her and everything that her team did for me. Any time I had any questions she would give me a quick phone call and clear anything up. Her confidence was very clear, it put me at ease in countless situations. She is an amazing lawyer and knows what she’s doing. Thankyou Lea! Also I cannot leave Mallory out of this! She works at the front desk and seriously saved me a few times when I wasn’t sure when and where certain things were going to be. Plus she made everything super simple and answered the phone every time I gave her a call. I’m very grateful for the many great things that this team did for me, just know you can never go wrong with Petro and Aston. :) Thankyou

Plus I want to say one more thing that I appreciate a lot. When I met with Mike and Lea I told them I changed some things in my life and ideas I had in my head because of the case at hand. Well they told me do not let this change anything about myself or hold myself back etc. They told me i’d be in good hands, and they proved that to be true. Against all odds..

I kept chasing what I wanted and pushed through turmoil. Those words helped me a lot. so I thank you for that.

Also thankyou god! 🙌🏼🙏🏼

I was battling a court case that had many layers to it. And both Mike and Leah from day one listened to me, went over all the tiny details of the reports and discovery. Kept in touch. Responded quickly. And they helped resolved and get my case dismissed. I would highly recommended hiring them as your law firm. They were great, they cared, they were extremely knowledgeable and I’m forever grateful.

Did my part getting 2 duis and I chose petro to represent me for my second one and did a miraculous job reducing my charges. I was looking at jail time and so so many fees and they took care of everything very professionally.

I cannot express how amazing these guys are! They helped me so much that I have no words! The weight they lifted off my shoulders was so great, I highly recommend them if you need a good lawyer on your side! Thank you guys!! 🙌🏼

They were very understanding and considerate of our case, and worked many long hours, researching every option to get the very best out come possible. I would highly recommend Petro & Aston for your defense. And would absolutely use them again should the need ever arise. “Thanks for a great job on our defense.”

Best attorney I know! Very professional, and willing to do whatever it takes to help you out. Most common response from somebody who knows him is, “ If I am ever in trouble he’s the one I’m calling”! I got word somebody was going to leave a bad review and decided to give an accurate take on the caliber of individual he truly is. Best guy I know…period! Everybody who knows him would see right through any negative garbage as a really sad attempt from someone crying out for attention!! If you find yourself in trouble and needing an attorney to defend you, look no further. Great rapport with most judges and others in his field. You want someone who knows the law backwards and forwards and every other way! Michael is the guy!!

Michael is very professional, easy to talk to and happy to explain things. He is highly respected among his peers. When I've mentioned his name in conversation, people are very quick to share the story of a time that he has helped them. I highly recommend him.

Great experience with Mike and his office, can’t say enough good things.  It was a challenging time for me, thankfully Mike took control and got things resolved quickly and better than I was expecting.
Mike is the best defense attorney in the business, just ask your local Police who they would have cover them, it’s Mike. If you’re in Utah County and need representation Mike is the guy to see.

Mike Petro is the best of the best! I cannot say enough good things about him. He is honest, professional, hard working, compassionate, informative, knowledgeable, and does his job better than anyone I’ve ever met. He is a champion for the underdog and has gone out of his way to help me when he didn’t need to. He is WELL respected by judges, juries, and local law enforcement. Anytime his name gets brought up people rave about how great he is and how much he helped them. You truly can’t find anyone better!!

Mike is an extremely competent attorney who is very, very well respected- his reputation truly speaks for itself. He bends over backwards for his clients and really cares. I can’t imagine there is another attorney who knows more about the law in Utah county. I couldn’t recommend Mike more!
Petro and his team are the BEST! His knowledge of the law and expertise is second to none.  Only premium experience.

Mike has helped me and my family with several matters over the years. There is no one else that I would trust to handle my legal matters. He has always been extremely present, prompt and professional.  He always answers my calls and questions and explains options that have allowed me to make the best decisions with all my legal needs.   I needed advice and guidance with a matter recently and Mike got my questions answered so the issue was resolved quickly. If you are in need of an attorney or guidance with legal matters I would give Mike Petro a call you’ll be happy you did!!

Mike and Leah represented my Dad in a very large case that was imperative to the future of our family. Mike and Leah were the best decision we ever made during this time and our family is so thankful for them. They showed extensive knowledge of the law that potentially saved my Dad. Not only did they provide him with a great defense, the manner in which they presented themselves during this process was admirable as well. I Would highly recommend Petro and Aston Law.

Brett walked us through a crazy process of insurance companies (and the lies) through medical care- don't trust that they really care about your situation-they are also a business that deals with people as their product. Without Brett and his team guiding us and responding to the overbearing, money grubbing system we would still be  suffering and paying out of pocket for our healthcare and recovery. I highly recommend Brett, his team and his firm to help anyone that gets into a situation and needs legal guidance. I will probably have life long symptoms, but now they are on the hook for my care. Great job Boulton/Flickinger/Robson.

We went to Brett because my wife was injured in an auto accident. I know many attorneys and many that handle personal injury cases. Brett resolved the case within one year and that is very fast for an injury case. He was able to convince the insurance company they must pay the full policy limit. My wife would rather have not been injured but at least she was able to get a large recovery because of Brett.

I can't recommend Brett Boulton highly enough!  If you're looking for a personal injury attorney, you've found the right place.  Brett is not only an incredibly skilled and knowledgeable lawyer, but he's also an exceptional person.  What truly sets him apart is his genuine empathy and his ability to connect with his clients on a personal level.  He doesn't just see you as a case; he sees you as an individual who's going through a difficult time, and he truly cares.  Beyond his kindness, Brett is incredibly competent and has surrounded himself with a staff that reflects his values – smart, capable, and just as kind as he is.  Going through a personal injury situation is stressful, but having Brett on your side makes all the difference.  He's a true advocate, and I'm so grateful to know where I can turn to for help when needed.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for the kind review, Kristen

reviews.text: I would give him 0 stars if I could. I've never seen a more greedy, money hungry, ambulance chasing lawyer in my life. He is the apitomy of lawyer jokes. They are heartbroken and you want to kick them while they are down.
